This is a keeper!  But took me 1.5 hours not one hour.  And I worked steadily!

Made it as written and it was excellent! Let the meatball mixture sit for about 10-15 min while I prepped the other ingredients before rolling into balls — definitely helped with the binding and marination. Will probably double the batch next time and freeze half for an even easier meal.
Try 3/4 c panko skip sugar did carrot instead
Great quick weeknight meal! We made as written, but added shiitake mushrooms (put in to lightly saute before adding sweet potato) and it adds a nice extra punch to the dish!

fantastic :) definitely a staple for me now. 
yes to using 3/4 to 1 cup of panko breadcrumbs and letting the mixture sit for 15ish minutes. I add a bit of tofu (about half of a 15oz container) to the meatballs, drained, pressed, and crumbled up, partly for some additional protein & partly to stretch out the ground chicken a bit, I think it works very well as a binder, haven't had them fall apart on me at all
Meatballs were flavorful and delicious. I served them separate from the curry. I would make these again and incorporate into other dishes!

Oh my goodness, this is soooo delicious. My son, who was cooking the curry, texted me at work and said “Mom, this is amazing, it’s going into the regular meal rotation!!” At our local Asian market, we found the best roux to be the Java curry, hot. It is rich and adds some spice but not too much.

After giving this recipe my first attempt (always stay very close to recipe on first try), I would try ¾ cup bread crumbs and opt for the spicier roux rather than the mild. Great suggestion on mixing in the roux in smaller bowl before moving to Dutch oven (I also need a much larger Dutch oven!)

All in all a massive success and this attempt garnered my 11yr old daughter to confess it is her all-time favourite dinner I’ve made! Wow!

Wow… fantastic. Only change was using 3.9 oz of curry roux ( half a pack of golden curry as others have mentioned)

Otherwise I followed the recipe to a tee and it was superb. Will definitely make again. Thanks Sohla El-Waylay

dissolving the curry brick in a mixture of hot water and sake made a big difference in bringing everything together once it was added to the pot. Used a chicken bone broth instead of water ....bit more protein and gave the curry nice body. Those mentioning the need for added panko might not have kneaded the panko with aromatics to make a paste before adding the chicken and egg because I did not face this issue .....good dish!

10/10! Not sure why other commenters said their meatball mixture was too wet. I had no trouble shaping them. And as noted, keeping my hands wet helped greatly in preventing the mix from sticking to them. Also, if you're struggling to get the Swiss chard cooked down, just cover the pot let steam help you, it practically disappears into the mix.

Never used curry roux before but WOW it's delicious (used Vermont Curry). This will be a great meal to make again when the weather gets cold.

Made this using the ground chicken 
YUM
going to try this again but using tofu instead of the chicken

This is my first Japanese curry but won’t be the last - family enjoyed it.  I used turkey instead of chicken for the meatballs and baked them instead of pan frying while preparing the rest of the dish.  I used a “mild” curry, but would try the medium next time as it could have used more heat.
Nobody liked.

I used 3/4 cup of panko and refrigerated the uncooked meatballs while I worked on the rest of the prep. No problems with cooking the meatballs, though I cooked them longer than directed. 16oz of spinach would have been 4 bunches. I bought 3. Washed 2 bunches (abt 8oz) and saw that was plenty. Actually added 2/3 of what I washed. It was still too much. When I tried folding the spinach in (one large handful at a time), some of the meatballs got mashed. I should have added the meatballs last maybe?
I don’t understand the high reviews, this was extremely bland- I think the brand of curry roux really matters here, any grocery store curry isn’t going to cut it.

absolutely increase to 3/4 cups of panko
chill ping pong size meatballs for 15 mins
don't be shy coating pan with oil
meatballs will release from pan, have faith! similar to browning a chicken thigh
follow recipe and method as written
I subbed sweet potatoes for carrots and red potatoes (both chunked)
also, meatballs will be moist, mix as directed, as opposed to some recipes that warn against over mixing

Absolutely love this dish. First time made it as written. Second time subbed carrots for the sweet potatoes and komatsuna (a type of Japanese green) for the spinach. Perfect low effort high reward dish!

Ran out of time and motivation so bought trader joes turkey meatballs. Browned them then sauted onion, added regular potato but added carrots.  The boiling ensured then are tender then the chopped up curry block and spinach last.  Whole family loved it.
Used 3/4c panko and let the meatballs mixture sit a spell before shaping. Came out perfect. I found this very salty and wish there had been more curry sauce. It was a massive hit with my hu

Used 3/4c panko (per everyone’s advice) and let the meatballs mixture sit a spell before shaping. They came out perfect. I found this very salty and wish there had been more curry sauce. However, it was a massive hit with my generally very picky husband though and he just asked me to make it again. Will experiment with using slightly less curry roux/more water. I also skipped the sugar and will add it back in next time to see if that mellows and balances out the perceived saltiness.

I was excited to make this Japanese curry.  The chicken "meatballs" were really good.  Love yams and greens too.  But the Golden cubes left a really unpleasant aftertaste and kind of ruined the dish. I would have preferred if the recipe included a list of spices to add fresh instead of using a commercial mix. Will add some red pepper flakes tonight to see if that helps - there's a lot leftover!
Fantastic recipe.  Made it exactly as written.  First time I had used the Japanese curry roux.  Didn’t even realize that was such an easy to find ingredient.    Will be using that again for sure!

Adding a new recipe to my “cozy semi-instant meatball recipe” canon - in good company with rice-a-roni meatballs (the OG recipe from the side of the beef RaR box). I let the meatballs sit in the fridge for 20 minutes or so before browning, I think that helped keep them together. Took others’ advice and whisked the roux with some hot broth before dumping it in. Garnished with sesame seeds - didn’t add much but they made it look nice. Incredibly delicious and satisfying and my first experience with Japanese curry - going to get this stocked in the pantry and looking forward to making a double batch when I have friends over this winter! P.S. I loved the sugar in the meatballs.
purchased  16oz of baby spinach leaves and only used 6oz.

Substituted ground turkey for the ground chicken and added juice of a full lime at the end as it needed some acid. Otherwise made as written- meatballs held together well with the 1/2 cup of panko. Use a good amount of oil and cooked first side a bit more, maybe 5mins, to brown and release from pan. Would make again (with the other half of my golden curry packet)!

Ingredient prep took 45 minutes, overall took 1.5 hours. Cooked meatballs unpleasantly soft (used 1 cup panko). Overall okay, but chunked chicken thighs are just as good and much easier. Won't repeat.
I used butternut squash and baby spinach. I also found the meatballs wet, and many of them broke apart, but I didn’t mind at all. I added a whole cup of water after adding the greens to thin it.

Delicious and easy. I used Impossible instead of chicken and the 1/2c of panko worked great. I also baked them in the oven instead of pan frying. I think it made everything come together quicker too since I was working on the sauce while the meatballs were cooking. I added 1/4lb of shiitake mushrooms also because I had them. I will definitely make this again.
I made this with impossible meat, gluten free bread crumbs and coconut aminos instead of soy sauce and it turned out amazing. Thanks for the recipe!
Thank you for this suggestions! Sounds delicious.@Mark P
Is it possible to cook the meatballs first and then add them to the curry mixture?
Yum! The meatballs would be great in other dishes too. I’m thinking of a banh mi style sandwich. I subbed carrots for sweet potatoes and used frozen spinach.

Delicious flavors! I prefer Japanese curry powder, so I incorporated it with the onions and oil, reduced the water to 2 cups, and added a potato starch slurry before adding the meatballs to the pan. Came out great!

This is delicious and my whole family loved it. I had ground turkey instead of chicken and it worked very well. I doubled the recipe to do some meal prep sharing with friends and everyone raved about this dish.
Absolutely delicious. I used a full thing of "Medium" curry sticks as well as a lot of the "Mild". First time ever making meatballs - so they were not pretty. But, the flavors were OUT OF THIS WORLD.
I tried the method of baking the meatballs per another reviewer's comment. I don't think this is wise to do as the meatballs will fall apart nor do they get that browned flavor.

Made as written except followed other readers' lead and doubled the panko.  While I suspect that the curry roux (S & B Golden Curry, medium hot) is intended for beef or lamb, it works with the chicken meatballs.  For future experiments, virtually any vegetable would probably work here.

This was really flavorful. You have to like curry obviously. My meatballs were a little sticky but they turned out ok though not quite round. I didn't have as much thick liquid as in the picture but I still felt the dish was outstanding. I served it with brown rice.

I know lots of people had issues with the breadcrumbs but it was fine for mine, you just really do have to knead! I used twice as much butternut squash as recommended and it was still good, delicious and fun to make :)

Another delicious Japanese curry recipe! I followed closely except I added a few dashes of fish sauce to the meatballs and threw in a handful of chopped cilantro in with the spinach. Served over udon noodles and we have plenty of leftovers for the week. Will make again! Served with some cold sake and it was the perfect Sunday meal.

I might just keep that meatball recipe and make them without the curry because they are that good! I was too lazy to shape them after work so I just used two spoons to portion the mixture into the pot, the craggly bits got extra crispy and brown :)

This one is a keeper.  I taught middle school in Japan in 1990’s and curry was a school lunch staple.  It’s economical, relatively easy and very comforting.  Based on comments I added about an extra 1/4 cup of panko and let the meatballs absorb the panko for about half an hour.  This recipe took me about an hour not counting the rest time for the meatballs.

This was good, but it needed a little tweaking to make it less sweet and give it more of a queso flavor. I added extra nutritional yeast, a bit of white vinegar to give it extra tang, and a can of green chilis. It was delicious and a good healthy alternative to normal cheese dip!
I was skeptical at first but this was surprisingly good! And healthy!
I add white miso in addition to the nooch to give it more umami depth and make it more cheese-like in flavor.
Not bad, but not queso. I can see using this as a sandwich spread if you keep it on the thicker side.

Tried this for the first time last week and I have been raving to vegan and non-vegan friends!

Made it as is and used it as a warm drizzle on some crispy black bean tacos I made. 

Saved the rest (about a quart) and used to make a very quick stovetop vegan mac and cheese through the week. Added some chopped pickled jalapeños and a touch of the pickle brine to the queso while the pasta boiled. 

I adjusted some seasonings to taste (added more nooch and a touch more MSG) but overall a really solid interpretation of a vegan queso. 

It’s now a part of my rotating vegan sauces that make meals more interesting!

I doubled the recipe and cooked everything in a 4 quart stockpot.  It was pretty good, I had to cook the mixture a lot longer to make the cashews soft enough.  Same texture as queso, tasted somewhat like cheese.  I used a combination of nutritional yeast powder and flakes.  I steamed some broccoli and poured a bunch of queso over it. I enjoyed it.  I'll make it again.
I liked it!

Made a version of this for some vegan mac and "cheese." I added three seeded, dried New Mexico chili peppers and around 2 tbs white miso. Only used one garlic clove (not sure why). Next time will use more. The chili peppers added some good zip. 

I made 12 oz of GF Jovial pasta and I have enough leftover sauce for at least another 12-oz box.

Overall, a good recipe. I'll make it again and will experiment some. Maybe more chili peppers. Might also try a little more oil.
This was actually pretty convincing as queso! It probably took like a quarter cup of salt though, haha (not really, but I aggressively salted and blended it like 5 times before it tasted right)

I make a variation of this every day (ordered something like 45 pounds of organic pea protein too to save in money, if not space!) 

(1) oat milk (any milk is fine obvi) 
(2) 2 dates 
(3) 1 ripe frozen banana 
(4) 1-2 scoops of pea protein (we have a ton right now, see alone,  so I’m drunk with power here) 
(5) cocao powder 
(6) splash of vanilla 
(7) salt! (Adds SO much!) and 
(8) mesquite bean powder (makes it taste more like a malt!) 

…. BLEND! Then add some (9) ice and blend some more! (This lets the ice not melt the first time around). 

Mesquite, and good dates, are more common in Phoenix, where we lived for a year. I recommend mesquite and especially good dates as much as I do NOT recommend Phoenix.
@MaddyChaddy dying lol. My sister moved to Phoenix about 6 months ago and is already making plans to escape.
Made this as written except I used regular milk instead of buttermilk because that’s what I had lying around and honestly, it was the best smoothie I’d had in a long time! Totally delicious.
Used regular milk and added a shot of espresso. Tasty! Didn't include flax seeds.
I make a version of this with low-fat Kefir instead of buttermilk. Recommend.

Loved this.
The dates add that idea of sticky, sweet glaze, slightly hardened after cooling. The ground flaxseed is the idea of the slightly gritty, crunchy inside of the baked roll’s cinnamon, sugar inside. I see both as almost necessary for the texture, which is truly a cinnamon roll in liquid form.

Taste-wise, I found I preferred to mix milk in with more yogurt instead of buttermilk, but I see that as a personal preference thing, not a recipe fault. For folks looking for a sweeter dessert smoothie adding brown sugar (or maple syrup!) might be necessary. I found that with a very ripe banana, no more sweetness is needed. What a fun smoothie!
What could "1/2 cup ice cubes" possibly mean? About one cube fits into my half cup measure. Would be nice to have this by #cubes or weight or something rational.
@skip I figured roughly a handful would work

@skip I think it’s critical to the recipe to have exactly 1/2 cup of ice cubes. So fill up a plastic 1/2 measuring cup with water, then pour the water into a larger container and weigh it. Then pour out the water, put the container back on the scale, and put ice cubes in the container until you get the same weight as before. Viola! Well, you may have to adjust for the difference between the volume of frozen versus liquid water. 

Or just eye ball it.
@Drew My morning chuckle! Thank you!
I used 2% milk and a room-temp banana and my kids swore I had blended a cinnamon bun in milk. It was delicious!
This reminds me of the date shakes we had in Palm Springs about 30 years ago.  They were delicious so we will certainly try this.
@Peter I had one of those 13 years ago and have been thinking about it ever since! Can’t wait to try this one.
@Celia. Is it still possible to get a Date Milkshake in Palm Springs?
I made it as the recipe instructed, but used normal milk. (I didn’t have buttermilk) The cinnamon is there, but feels odd. This tastes weird, never making this again. 2 out of 5

Just got back from Palm Springs last night and wanted to keep the date shake thing going. I made exactly as written - delicious! My dates are small so I might put 3 in next time. As for the comments that the dates don't puree, this is the beauty of the date shake, the little bits of date you get to chew. I love it!

Really struggled to get the dates blended in. First try they were mostly whole. Second try I chopped them first and there were still lots of date pieces at the bottom—not well integrated like in the pic. I assume the testers used a high performance blender like a Vitamix. My cheap, lower-powered version didn’t seem up to the task
Try (chopping and) freezing dates, lightly dusted ground flax seeds or cocoa powder ahead of time. Makes blending easier.
Made as directed and it was too tangy and not sweet enough.  Added a touch of brown sugar and it was good.

I made it exactly as written and it was delicious - others note leaving out the flax seed or using regular milk instead, but the former is definitely giving it nuttiness, and the latter tang, so it really wouldn't be the same.

Made as directed and found it had a tangy taste and overall was not unpleasant but not amazing either.  It lacked richness too, and I’m not fond of doctoring reasonably healthy smoothies with extra sweeteners just for flavor.  Ok but not a keeper for me.

My go-to smoothie:

3/4 cup whole milk
1 ripe banana
1 T peanut butter
1 T cacao (unsweetened)
1 date (optional)
2 scoops plain whey protein (optional)
6 ice cubes
you should add some frozen spinach. It tastes like nothing and adds so many nutrients

I am dairy free (womp womp) and so used almond milk instead of buttermilk and kite hill Greek style almond milk and soy yogurt and it was still an unbelievable smoothie. I threw in a little bit of cacao flavored protein powder and a tablespoon of almond butter and it was to die for. I think the salt really makes it!
Pretty good. Some tahini takes it to eleven.

Man oh man did this make me slurp! It was so daggone delicious! highly recommend. Now the tanginess of the buttermilk was wonderful IMHO but I could see why some of y'all wanted to tame the tang a little. For me though this was a total zippedeedoodah; can't wait to make for ma and sneak in some Benefiber!

If you like a thicker smoothie try only 3/4 cup buttermilk. 

You can make a buttermilk substitute with 1 cup milk and 1 tbsp white vinegar.

@NC You could, but it really will just taste like sour milk. That approach is best for baked goods when you need an acid to react with baking soda. In this case, yogurt thinned with water would be a better substitute since you get that complex cultured flavor.
I used to make this recipe as a healthy breakfast smoothie. When my husband tried it, he asked me if I was sure this was "healthy" because it tasted "sinful!"
pears instead of bananas. Kefir. Sweetener as needed

This is SO good. I think the buttermilk really is key for the tangy taste that makes it reminiscent of a cinnamon roll with a cream cheese glaze. Really recommend folks try it with.
I did need to make one sub -- I am allergic to banana, so used steamed, frozen cauliflower to make up the bulk of the smoothie (bonus: it has a more neutral/not noticeable taste). I also added vanilla protein powder (partly for the protein boost, partly to add some sweetness the cauliflower doesn't have).

Blitzed half a recipe in the Nutribullet, then used it as the base for overnight oats. I needed to soak, then chop the date, before the blender could do anything with it…does taste just like a cinnamon roll. Great idea - will do again.
I had some leftover buttermilk and used it in this smoothie. What a great way to use leftover buttermilk! I did not know buttermilk in a smoothie could taste this good!
Made as written. This is ridiculously good
Buttermilk (full fat) is not optional. But used walnuts instead of flax. Kids loved it.
Best smoothie recipe ever! I replaced the buttermilk with 1/2 c kefir and 1/2 c almond milk. I didn’t have a frozen banana so used an unfrozen one. Phenomenal!

Excellent with plant milk of any kind (oat, soy, almond, or hemp), which will help avoid cholesterol, saturated fat, IGF1, and, now, bird flu virus (H5N1), found in cow milk. Come on NYT! Dairy is NOT healthy.

For everyone aghast that the NYT would specify low-fat anything, buttermilk is naturally low fat. It’s the milk leftover from butter production. If you find a “whole milk” buttermilk, that means that fat has been added back in. 

My family all really enjoyed this smoothie! Yum. For myself, I might do half buttermilk, half almond milk or something next time.

@McKenzie Although that was how buttermilk originated, in the American South they use “buttermilk” to describe whole sweet milk that’s soured by lactic acid producing bacteria. Buttermilk is both the watery tangy leftover liquid from butter making and the whole milk based drink.

I followed this recipe exactly and it was delicious. The buttermilk adds a lot of flavor to the smoothie. Made a great breakfast! I'll try using some protein powder or maybe add a little almond butter next time to make it more filling.. but it's perfect as it is.

I didn't have butter milk so I made my own by putting a splash of vinegar into the milk. I also did not have flax seeds. The rest was followed. This turned out delicious! I will definitely be making this again. Possibly with a shot of espresso!
Made this today. Refreshing and cure for a sweet tooth. I used regular milk. The salt is a great addition.
Delicious! I was scared of the full cup of buttermilk so I opted for 1/2 C buttermilk and 1/2 C oat milk. Just enough tang for me.

I didn't have buttermilk so I made my own, using the cheater method of 1 tbsp of white vinegar to 1 cup of milk. (This also works with plant milks.)

The whole smoothie was absolutely delicious and I didn't need a quart of buttermilk to go to waste in the fridge.

This was so tasty! I have had it twice in two days. I made it as directed and also made it with plain Greek yogurt and whole milk in place of the buttermilk- I prefer the latter. 
No need for a high speed blender here, as the little bits of dates and flax make it even more reminiscent of a cinnamon bun.

Loved this. No buttermilk so used combo of almond milk and 2% milk. Used chia seeds instead of flax, and added 1/2 cup Greek yogurt for protein. Everything else as directed.  No lie, this tasted just like a delicious cinnamon bun without the cloying sweetness and carb overload. Will be in my regular rotation going forward.

I was skeptical of this recipe but holy moly I was wrong. Subbed Greek yogurt for the buttermilk to get more protein in and swapped hemp hearts for the flax seeds because I didn’t have any. I also added some powdered peanut butter on top. It was completely delicious. I’ll be adding this to my rotation.

I made this with whole milk because it was what I had and regular dates from Aldi's because it was what I had. I added a spoonful or two of Greek yogurt into the smoothie before mixing. It was pretty good. The dates didn't get super smooth; I've heard soaking them in hot water for a few minutes can help with that. This was good though.
I add almond butter and then it’s pretty much the Second Cup date and almond smoothie, which I love when I want a not too fruity smoothie.

As a Sohla stan I will always give her concoctions a try. I had all the ingredients, to boot! This was a true diversion from my usual berries and greens smoothies, and a revelation. Sweet, nutty, tangy, with the warmth of cinnamon and the occasional chewy bit of date. Yum!
I omitted the salt. Added half cup of walnuts for fiber and espresso shot for caffeine. Delicious, despite my non-artistic "yogurt swirl."
swapped buttermilk for kefir (just what I had in the fridge), I really enjoyed the tang!
Delightful! Made with whole milk kefir since that's what I had on hand and it was the perfect amount of tang.
Great with plain Kefir in place of buttermilk for a little tang and health boost. Don’t miss the salt - it definitely enhances the flavor. One of the best smoothies I’ve had in a good while!

I used oat milk here, which worked just fine. Per another commenter, I added additional ice after my initial round of blending and that really thickened the whole thing up nicely. This is delicious and tastes just like a cinnamon roll. Next time, I'll add a scoop of protein powder or maybe even some silken tofu just to get a little protein. This is getting added to the regular rotation of morning smoothies.

This smoothie is out of control good. I was going to skip out on the buttermilk and then saw how much protein it has in it, which sold me. I have a hard time eating in the morning, and I wake up craving this smoothie. I am going to add some espresso powder next to see how it changes things.
I can’t believe how good this tastes even without any sweetener. It really does taste like a cinnamon bun, and there’s nothing that’s outright "bad for you" in it. 10/10, no notes.
Made recipe as written— one of the best smoothies I’ve ever had.
I used oat milk, a scoop of protein powder, added a tablespoon of chia, and substituted cinnamon for pumpkin pie spice. I also reduced to a half of a frozen banana. This was delicious.

This was delicious and I’m going to be honest, I was a little skeptical at first thinking it would not be that sweet. It was so good, I might make it everyday! I think my picky kids would even love it.

I made this with with 1 substitution: peanut butter instead of flax seeds because buying two specialty ingredients for a smoothie was enough for me.  I thought it was tasty and creamy a nice way to start a summer day, but generally unremarkable.  (And unfortunately it reminds me of the sad banana and peanut butter powder smoothies I ate for breakfast in college) 
Now that I have the dates I'd make it again but I would make it again but subbing diluted  yogurt for the buttermilk
I used whole milk. Added vanilla protein and used hemp seeds instead of flax. Wow so good! Salt makes the dates pop off, so don’t skip that!
My husband made this for me. The smoothie is very tasty
This is one of my faves. I love it as is but if Im out of dates I find that a handful of raisins works too.

I really love this. Have yet to purchase buttermilk so can’t really speak to the recipe exactly. In place of buttermilk I use plain yogurt mixed/oat milk then follow recipe. I drink this way too often. Yum.
Did not, in all transparency, taste like a cinnamon roll. But tasty nonetheless! I added protein powder for a little extra boost.
@Katastic I agree, it was tasty, but had nothing to equate it with a cinnamon roll.

This is so so so good. I love how there are little bits of un blended dates. One thing I found was I tried this with my immersion blender and almost broke it. From then on I have had to use my standing blender which is a little more of a pain to clean but worth it. I like to add a spoonful of peanut butter and a scoop of chocolate protein powder and a scoop of vanilla protein powder. Sometimes I put spinach in it and although the color can be off putting, you can’t even taste the spinach! This flavor combo is so versatile, I have made an overnight oats version with it. Perfect for when you get tired of chocolate or banana peanut butter smoothies, but want a similar vibe.

What a great use of leftover buttermilk! (I ALWAYS have extra buttermilk after using it for a recipe because the perfect amount of buttermilk is never to be found in a grocery store.) Thank you for helping me achieve my goal of 0 food waste!
Wish I knew what to sub for banana.....
Didn't have very much flavor despite making exactly as instructed
I am allergic to bananas, and almost every smoothie recipe calls for them either mashed or frozen. Substitute suggestions, please and thanks?
I'm also allergic to bananas so I use avocados instead to give similar texture.
I hate bananas.  I just leave them out, no subbing anything.
frozen cauliflower--just by cauliflower rice and put it in there...even healthier.  You might need another date!
Very tasty and pretty sweet. Nice way to use up buttermilk, I only had a half a cup but the cinnamon roll vibes were still shining.

Very tasty! Try this even if you are skeptical! It's really good. (I used half yogurt and half buttermilk.)

Even though I used a good quality blender, I could not get the dates completely pureed, so there were some flecks of date in my drink. Didn't bother me, but it might bug some people. I don't know if pre-soaking the dates might help, to soften them more.
This is - DELICIOUS!

Great smoothie and tastes so much like a cinnamon roll! I didn’t have buttermilk so increased plain Greek yogurt to 1/2 c. Added some spinach and collagen powder for more protein, then cold water for liquid. No extra sweetener, it is already quite sweet. Finally, tossed in a few toasted pecans at the end because I love a little crunch!
DELICIOUS! Used chia seeds instead of flax. 10/10

Super yummy!
Not reallllly a cinnamon roll. But a really yummy date smoothie!

Extremely delicious and easy to make! I followed the recipe almost exactly,doubling it for two servings, using one frozen and one unfrozen  banana and tossing in six dates. Husband loves it and so did I!
Delicious! If you have a vitamix, it really pulverizes both the dates and ice. Used more frozen banana and cinnamon.  A hit!

Made this on a snowy day and it was pretty good! My blender is old so I was left with some date chunks still in mine but I’m not mad because they add a fun texture. I left out the flax seed as I didn’t have any and it was still good, although a little watery. Tastes like cinnamon and banana :)
My son calls this the “vanilla milkshake” smoothie. 10/10 would recommend.

The dates did not blend in well and the smoothie ended up more sour than expected.  I was using a spoon to scoop up the dates at the bottom of the glass and then I understood the hype better.  I added espresso before I got to the end.
Sounded delicious and I was interested so I clicked, but the photo of the glass with the yogurt decoration sealed the deal: I am trying this :D

Don't skimp on the buttermilk! Use whole farm fresh buttermilk for the real taste. Don't use pre ground cinnamon... dry fry a few sticks and grind them in a spice grinder while hot. Use extra large eggs and add an extra cube of ice to compensate for the thickness of whole buttermilk. I make my own vanilla out of bourbon and vanilla pods... try it!
Accidentally made this with CUMIN instead of cinnamon and somehow it still tasted delicious… absolutely fool proof!!!!!
Very good. I used three dates. Next time it will be five. Other than that I followed the recipe
This is a really unique smoothie, well thought out. Very satisfying.
I enjoyed this. I made it exactly as written. I wouldn’t go out of my way to buy buttermilk to make this, but it’s definitely a good way to use up leftover buttermilk from other recipes.
I used 2% milk. I found the taste to be mild. Added 2 more dates (4 dates total), dash more cinnamon and it was perfect!!!
I used unsweetened soy milk, and a slice of silken tofu, to increase the plant protein.  I also add some psyllium husk and plant sterol powder.  Didn't use flax seed or ice cubes.  Tasted very good.
Somehow, I ran out of cinnamon and I used pumpkin pie spice as a substitute last minute. I think it (or apple pie spice) would make for a really good fall smoothie!

The drink in the photo sure looks prettier than the real thing.  I'm not sure how they got the color of the smoother to appear so dark...and to provide such nice contrast to the swirls of yogurt?  And I even added twice the amount of dates, seeing as a mere two dates didn't really make sense to me, seeing as it's called a 'Cinnamon Date' smoothie.  Either way, very tasty, and quite sweet ....maybe too sweet?

This smoothie intrigued me when it was suggested one day. I saved it to try when I next had dates on hand. I finally made it last week and my kids loved it. They said it reminded them of a blended, frozen king cake. We've made it a couple times since then too and it's always great. We do add extra cinnamon, though.

I like cinnamon buns but even though I used less than the called for amount of cinnamon I found it a little too cinnamon forward and almost astringent - that may be due to the type of cinnamon I have though. Otherwise it was nice!

I swapped out the buttermilk for kefir that I got from my local farmers market. Other than that I followed the recipe as is and it is DELICIOUS. It tastes just like a cinnamon roll with cream cheese icing. Next time I may add an extra date. My only question is- why or how does the picture look the way it does? lol.

This was pretty good and a similar flavor to a cinnamon bun. But it is very sweet and syrupy. I enjoyed it, but probably wouldn’t make it again. Maybe cut the dates and banana by half? Very creative idea and pretty finishing touch to add the swipes of yogurt on the glass!
Thoughts on a banana substitute?

both my toddler and I loved this one. we doubled the dates for extra sweetness.  my only complaint is that the dates didn't blend up smoothly but that is probably an operator-error. Instead of buttermilk I used normal milk with a splash of white vinegar.
what would be a good sub for the flax?  Im not gonna buy flax seeds for a smoothie recipe.  Peanut butter? Sunflower seeds? almond flour? Just skip it?

This came out a lot thinner than I’m used to for smoothies. Sweetness level was perfect. Next time will try it with plain Greek yogurt and protein powder. I think cottage cheese would also add a salty tang.
I’ve been making this with dairy free-yogurt and oat milk instead of buttermilk and adding cinnamon almond butter for more protein. So good!

Really nice! I used full fat buttermilk and subbed the flax for some peanut butter. It really does remind you of a cinnamon roll! My one regret is that the overripe banana I used gave it a kind of acidic, cloying sweetness. Would def make again
Doesn't the recipe call for flax seed?
@ML Yes, it does. I used peanut butter because that’s what I had on hand. Although after making it again with flax seeds, I think flax seeds do make the smoothie taste more cinnamon roll like
Used a few strawberries since I was out of bananas. Couldn’t even taste them. This was seriously so good! I’m always amazed when something that seems so straightforward is so delicious.
I have a cheap blender which struggles with blending the dates - I found that if I pit and freeze them beforehand they blend a little easier.
Try chopped dates.  That's what I have so that's what I'm going to do!

Hello all, we loved this cake; however, it only rose to 1 inch tops. More like brownie than cake. I followed instructions to a t.  I followed ingredients list and measured carefully…with one exception. I used a plant-based “butter” instead of the real thing because I had it available. Do you think that was culprit or something else?  Thankful for your comments.

Absolutely love this. Definitely don’t understand the people saying it’s not rich enough or not sweet enough or lacks flavor. Crazy. 

It really does remind me of a cinnamon bun with cream cheese frosting. It’s hard to get medjool dates here, we mostly just get California dates so I used three since they’re much smaller but I think it’s would honestly be sweet enough without them. I made exactly as written but added collagen and creatine since I’m trying to incorporate them into my diet but don’t have a lot of ways to do so. Having this as a before bed snack feels like a true treat! 

I do think you could leave out the ice cubes and not notice, it’s not very thick with current proportions and doesn’t need any thinning in my opinion. I might try it with kefir next time just for the added health benefits but I feel like it won’t be as good!

WOW I went into this not knowing what to expect. I had some bananas that were about to go bye bye then I saw this recipe. This is amazing. I had no idea it would be this good and I know exactly what’s in it.
Now I need more over ripe bananas.

Make healthy 
Buttermilk -> fair life milk
I love this recipe, I feel like I’m drinking a milkshake! I use a few dashes of cardamom instead of the cinnamon and 1 tbs of peanut butter instead of the flax seeds.
Wow, I happened to have some cinnamon rolls in my kitchen when I made this, so I could compare back-and-forth. There is a clear similarity in both taste and texture. Love it.
The cinnamon is the star in this smoothie -- so good. I made it with coconut milk. Such simple ingredients but so good for you. Thank you!
The smoothie was delicious using the exact recipe. I will make it again without any modifications.

This smoothie is my go to! So good. I do have a word of warning. Not only is this dangerously good, but it’s downright dangerous if not consumed properly. If you choose to garnish with a lovely sprinkle of cinnamon… DRINK WITH A STRAW.

*if you don’t use a straw you risk the fate of inhaling cinnamon and trust me, you don’t want that experience.

Very tasty treat, and super sweet with dates and bananas, even with buttermilk. Next time, I’ll use more yogurt to balance that out. Yet, it is definitely a dessert rather than a smoothie, which I think of as a meal.
What would someone use instead of banana? I’m allergic and am so sad.
Tried once with only 1/4c buttermilk and the rest reg milk, and it was nice.  Today I tried with the full amount of buttermilk and it is BONKERS delicious.  Follow this as written, it’s perfect!
Total fave; great tang and almost rich. Yogurt on glass essential.

My one year old tasted it and declared “cookie!”. It’s very good. I used nonfat Greek yogurt and a little whole milk to thin it in place of buttermilk. I had trouble with the dates blending properly but used an immersion blender. Flax seed essential.
I'm not normally a sodium hawk, but 1231 mg sounds like A LOT!

- 1/4 cup buttermilk and 3/4 cup regular milk, perfectly tangy for me. 
- a spoonful of tahini, which increases the richness
- date paste, which blends more smoothly
Side note: I guess substitutes are nice to know in a pinch, but there’s a reason why she used buttermilk!
I make this exactly as written and i must have made it 20 times. I love this smoothie. I buy my dates at Costco...fresh and soft so they blend well.
Oh that does taste like a cinnamon roll!  Used non-dairy milk & yogurt b/c those are easier on my digestion but still found it very decadent smoothie.
Really nice - I'd also recommend a shot of espresso (helps melt some of the ice if you have sensitive teeth like me) and a drizzle of maple syrup for a little extra sweetness.
According to Google, half a cup of “standard” ice cubes should weigh 60-70 grams.

stats.Total Time: 45 minutes plus at least 1 hour’s chilling

comments.commentBody: My monocle popped out while reading this. I’ll need to buy more houses on Boardwalk to afford this recipe.
grocery store caviar isn't expensive

I've made this for years and always use less expensive lumpfish caviar black or red, sometimes even salmon or trout roe). Makes a great impression for a crowd.
I save the good stuff to be eaten as it should be, with toast points or blini and other garnishes optional.

@Joe
I make mine with fake caviar-comes in a jar, about $5. Works just fine. (look for this in Russian, Slavic, or Middle Eastern stores.)
Wow ! This is the almost exact recipe from the First (Red) Women’s Exchange of Memphis, TN cookbook from years ago! My Mom had this every year at holiday parties and it was soooooo good!
The stuff legends are made of! My Christmas dinner companions were fighting over who would get to lick the bowl. Thank you, Sohla.
My uncle is famous for his caviar pie and his recipe is very similar but uses scallions instead of chives. We serve it with wheat thins because the sweet nuttiness balances the rest of the flavors.

I just made this as a triple decker layer pie (think layer cake), with each layer a new type of caviar that is progressively more expensive and exquisite. The hardest part was pairing with sufficiently delicate and appropriately rare bottles of champagne. By the time we finished, I served a standalone caviar tasting on great aunt’s mother of pearl spoons, they came over when she transferred her assets out of Swiss vault. My guests loved this experience. Highly recommend.
@Bill Whoa and wow! Thumbs up. How did the caviar fare when layered?
@Bill this sounds incredible! And inspired.

For previous comments, so many type Os!
That is Chevre' cheese to replace the cream cheese. Rinsing the shallots did not diminish the flavor. I did not have chives, so substituted finely minced scallion greens.
To Paul D - it's very good made a day ahead, just don't put the caviar on until close to serving time. I'm making mine right now for NYE tomorrow!

this is a fantastic idea!…i wonder if there could be a lightly buttered bellini “crust” on the bottom?
maybe extravagant for a large party but sweet for. party of two with a bottle of nice V….happy new years!

Made it for Christmas - easy, relatively economical (the recipe notes are accurate: you can get away with a less-expensive caviar with this dish), and an easy win. Party guests raved and it went fast.

Only tip I'd add is to taste the mixture carefully for salt: the caviar I used was on the salty side, and so were the chips I used. A delicious salt-bomb, but next time I'll be more careful.

Very similar idea to mom’s recipe (Columbus OH, c 70s) but the lower level was pate, then the cream cheese, iced with caviar… with rings of minced onion, minced yolks, diced egg white, then finally a layer of curly parsley… very pretty on the buffet, with bremner wafers… May need to try this for new years! Ty.
Can this be made the day before serving?
@Paul D I would say to make it up to the point before adding the caviar. Just wrap tightly in plastic and refrigerate so it doesn’t dry out. Add the caviar before serving.
You won’t be disappointed making this recipe! We couldn’t get enough!

Oops, previous comments. For lactose intolerance, goat yogurt instead of sour cream. Chevre instead of cream cheese.
 Gently mixed the egg yolk mixture, shallots and finely chopped egg whites because I misread the instructions and it turned out fine. Served in a shallow bowl, made it easier to transport and serve. Topped with Salmon roe before serving. A giant hit with the crispy veg and assorted thin crackers. Next time, will be easier.
delicious as a dip with potato chips. almost even better slathered onto a thick slice of toasted sourdough for breakfast.

I've been making this star of the show for years. Because it contains so many ingredients, it doesn't suffer if you use a less expensive caviar (lumpfish, either black or red, salmon caviar, American Golden). I always serve it with lightly toasted baguette slices. And good champagne or top shelf iced vodka, of course!
Could I double the recipe and put it in a springform pan?

Trout roe typically costs between $40 and $65 for 4 oz.  Since this feeds 12 people, the cost per person is between $3 and $5.  This is an affordable way to make food for a gathering seem luxurious even when you're on a budget.
Nothing in the world beats sturgeon caviar with blini and sour cream. And a glass of chilled vodka.

Made this with Imperia Caviar as I feared the mixture of flavors would overpower grocery store caviar which tends to be lower quality. It was excellent, and sparked a full 30 minutes of conversation at my dinner. Definitely not too expensive and delicious (if you know how find quality ingredients).
Made for Thanksgiving gathering. It was delicious. Served with potatoe chips, taro chips and blini. Yummy and a big treat.

Made this for a Christmas appetizer (feast of the seven fishes)— everyone was skeptical, but they LOVED it. Made with salmon roe to keep costs down, assembled in a glass pie pan (let’s not get tooooo fancy), and served with salt and vinegar kettle chips. I second the recommendation to enjoy with an ice-cold martini, my pref is Grey Goose with an extra twist!

A big appetizers hit! For lactose intolerance I substituted cheered and goat yogurt.
For me, I made and served it in a shallow bowl, which made it easy to transport and serve. I misread the instructions and laid the yolk and butter mixture down, and then patting on the prepped onions(oops). So I just added the chopped whites and tossed all together, then pressed down, and proceeded with the creamy ingredients. I used Salmon roe. Served with breakfast radishes, sliced cakes, and very crisp crackr

I had this at a catered holiday party one year and when I saw it what it cost when planning my own party (almost $100), I decided to make it myself. Found this recipe. Very easy and delicious. Sometimes, for home use, I just put all the layers on a flat bowl and don't worry about the looks. I have also just made the egg salad with sour cream topping and green onions, skipping the caviar. Makes a great sandwich.

My mother used to make something similar as a dip. She would use a very thick sour cream and mix in the grated zest of a lemon and fresh lemon juice, finely chopped shallots, and some garlic salt and celery salt. She would let that sit overnight for the flavours to mingle. She potted the dip into a festive bowl, added the chopped hard boiled eggs on top (whites and yolks separated for colour), and then added lumpfish caviar on top. She served it with Carr’s water crackers.
Back in the day, this caviar dish was a star at my annual Christmas open house! It is that good - and it looks spectacular on the table.

My dad introduced us to caviar with a simple version of this - shape a disc of cream cheese and place in the center of a "fancy" plate. Surround it with a ring of minced onion, a ring of chopped egg white, and a ring of smashed egg yolk. Top the cream cheese with the caviar and serve with your choice of crackers or toasts.

Served with matzo so as not to interfere with the flavors. It was a very fun and festive snack to come home to after dancing all evening. I under seasoned the egg and cream cheese layers, reasoning that the ikura would be plenty salty, but they could have used a little more.
It was great and looked so festive and special. I will make it again but will cut the cc/sour cream layer in half.
The Junior League of San Francisco published a cookbook circa 1978-81 that had this recipe. I save it for  holidays and always , always get rave reviews.
I'm going to use small individual ramekins to reduce use of caviar. I also might top a few with smoked salmon.
Could this be done in reverse in a bowl or mould and then inverted onto a platter for a prettier look?

PS - I tried this when I made the recipe for a wine night with friends yesterday and it worked well. I lined an 8in round bowel with plastic wrap, lightly sprayed with non-stick. Then I added the layers in reverse, starting with the cream cheese mixture, then shallots, then the egg. Let set in the fridge and inverted onto a plate when ready to serve. 
Delicious recipe, even if you just use the more available lumpfish roe
Just wondering… Why do you rinse the shallot after chopping it?
It cuts the 'oniony-ness' by rinsing away the sulfer.
I love this for breakfast on rye toast!
I made this, used decent caviar but the result was  meh. Nice presentation though.

I make this with not too expensive black caviar and salmon roe.  It's always a hit. The recipe I use is a little simpler.  Put the hard boiled eggs (cold) in a food processor with 4 tablespoons butter.  No fuss, no muss.  I use a smallish springform pan instead of a ring mold
Good but everything overpowered the caviar for me.
How is this with just the cheese and caviar layer, or an alternative to the egg layer? It looks so festive for a party, but I’m not the biggest fan of boiled eggs.
Perhaps it was me but the egg cook time was not quite sufficient for me - would have preferred a firmer yolk for this one to hold its shape better!
How do you get this out of the mold again, if the caviar is on top?  It’s not hard.

You have probably seen them as Lotus brand Biscoff cookies. Speculoos cookies are Belgian Speculaas and are traditionally made with spices, butter, and brown sugar and baked until completely crispy giving them a very caramelised flavour. They make a "cookie butter" spread from the ground up cookies; it's kind of like peanut butter. The Lotus Biscoff spread is the most common but stores like Walmart and Trader Joe's have homebrand versions.

For everyone asking, speculoos (or speculaas) is a type of spiced shortbread cookie. The brand here in the US is usually Biscoff, and you're likely to find the spread or butter at Trader Joe's.
An internet search seems to say that Biscoff is a type of speculoos cookie and that you can make speculoos cookie butter with it if you can't buy the premade variety locally.

For those asking about "cookie butter": It is like peanut butter/nutella but made of speculoos cookies. The most common brand is Lotus Biscoff Cookie Butter but there are a lot of dupes available. If you are in the USA Trader Joe's sells one literally called "Speculoos Cookie Butter" (dark green label) but the original Lotus brand is called Biscoff speculoos spread/butter (red and white label) is what Sohla is using and it is fairly easy to find in Europe, North America, Australia, UK etc.
Speculoos is internationally more known under the brand name Biscof - the brand also has the biscof spread or speculoospasta, which is I guess what she means with cookie butter
Cookie butter is Biscoff Spread? Pretty sure it is a thing. Trader Joe’s also has Speculoos Cookie Buttet Spread https://www.traderjoes.com/home/products/pdp/speculoos-cookie-butter-096761
Cookie butter is the spiced ginger cookie mixed with butter and sugar to form a "butter."  It's available at Walmart.  It used to be at Trader Joe's and appears to have been discontinued.

Speculoos is a Belgian/Dutch cookie similar to a graham cracker or digestive biscuit. Cookie Butter is a spread made out of ground speculoos and oil with extra fat and sugar. It also comes from Belgium/the Netherlands, but has been popular in the US for nearly a decade. The original brand, which is widely available in North America and Europe, is Biscoff.

@Deborah T I can get Biscoff cookies and Biscoff Spread "kakkräm" at my local Willy's, so yes, it is available in Sweden.
In the USA - during the holiday season (aka now), Aldi has speculoos cookies in many shapes for very reasonable prices.

Speculoos Cookie Butter Recipe Source: https://www.liveeatlearn.com/wprm_print/how-to-make-speculoos-cookie-butter Ingredients 1 8.8 oz package Biscoff cookies 250 g ½ cup water 120 mL 2 Tbsp brown sugar ¼ tsp cinnamon ¼ tsp salt ½ cup coconut oil solid Instructions: Make Crumbs: Add cookies to a blender or food processor and pulse until they become fine crumbs. Make Paste: Add the water and continue blending into a smooth paste. Add all remaining ingredients and blend until smooth.
