The raw broccoli is great! When you add the hot/warm quinoa, it softens the broccoli ever so slightly and it’s delicious. I didn’t have pecans so I added pumpkin seeds. It was a hit!

Made exactly as written. Wouldn't change a thing. I was amazed at how the dressing softened and enhanced the raw broccoli. I allowed it to remain dressed for about an hour, then added everything else. That kept the apple and pecans crisp, giving a nice variety of textures. The dressing was delightful, the meal hearty enough to not need a separate entree. We have plenty leftover for lunch, for which I'm very happy!

In my opinion, a little heavy on the quinoa ... which makes all the other flavors/colors not so vibrant. Cheddar is definitely not necessary -- I agree with the other reviewer that feta might make a more interesting addition. Definitely parboil the broccoli -- for color and texture. Raw broccoli is indigestible!
Having seen other shoppers sneeze and cough before picking up broccoli and other similar vegetables, I rinse and steam or cook it before consumption.
Made just as is, and it is delicious. Good quality cheddar important.
Just made this exactly as written other than subbing red wine vinegar for the apple cider vinegar. absolutely delicious! Do not par boil. The broccoli - the dressing does the trick.

Am eating this now as I type!  I agree that only 1/2 cup of quinoa is needed. I’m vegan so left off the cheese(not a big fan of vegan cheese) and roasted pepitas cuz no pecans on the house. Also- no apple as I didn’t have one. Thought the dressing was bland so I added a little bit of chili crunch which was hanging out next to my Dijon. I love it with a bit of heat!!!!
Briefly Rinse quinoa in a sieve under running water before cooking to avoid bitter taste.

Made the recipe as written using red quinoa and smoked cheddar. Very pretty, fresh, crunchy salad. I would say the red quinoa helped pick up the visual interest and the smoked cheddar added a bit more nuance to the flavor profile.

I thought this was very good. I made it as written. I found that it made a huge quantity—more like 6-8  . It calls for a lot of quinoa and broccoli. I have started halving recipes now b/c it’s just my husband and I. If it’s just two of you, definitely cut this in half. I’ve been eating this twice a day for 3 days now……

All I have to say is..Make this! Perfect combination of sweet and tart and fresh flavors! I did indeed eat it with a spoon....and fully intend to put this in front of anyone I meet. Perfect for a side to seafood....or as a stand alone chopped salad!
Used feta instead of cheddar and added grapes, sunflower seeds, and blueberries, omitted dried cranberries bc I didn’t have any. Delicious. The dressing is excellent!
Made the recipe as posted (although subbed toasted walnuts for pecans because that's what I had on hand). Tasted after cooking and it was good. Next day for lunch, it was incredible as a cold salad.

My family loved this from the get go.  Easy to chop everything that needs chopping in a food processor with the grater blade.  This would be a great salad without the quinoa as well.  Will definitely be making this again and again.

I had an excess of beets and no apple, so used finely chopped raw beets in lieu of apple. Skipped the cheese, nuts, and cranberries.  Delicious.  My non-veggie loving self and spouse both ate it up with enthusiasm for several days.  And yes, the vinegar "cooks" the broccoli.
I meal prep my weekday lunches -- would appreciate anyone's thoughts on the staying power of this salad! With a side of roasted chicken, it would equal a great meal!
Great, even with some tweaks. My lemon was bad and apparently I had no AC vinegar, so I added some white wine vin + white balsamic. I'll definitely be making this again for lunches!

This salad is fast, healthy and tasty. I'm in the raw broccoli camp. The dressing is flavorful, the quinoa is a nutrition boost, and there are lots of options to change ingredients based upon what is available. I made this the first time to go with baked salmon fillets for a company dinner and then had it the next morning for breakfast on top of cottage cheese. This is a perfect cooking assignment for a young or inexperienced cook.

Delicious. Notes: 
--Like someone else here, I thought there would be too much quinoa for the salad, so, once it was cooked, I scooped out two cups, which will be saved to make quinoa chocolate cake. 
--I'm not a fan of raw broccoli, but if you chop the broccoli very fine, it's great!
--I used the juice of one whole lemon, as the one I had had extremely thick skin and not a whole lot of juice. 
--Had dried tart cherries on hand, so threw some of those in, too. I will definitely make this again.
Delicious.  I used a combo of cheddar and feta.  Also instead of quinua I used Trader Joes Harvest Blend.  Might try adding roasted butternut next time.

Very very good. I added a grated carrot and subbed raisins for cranberries and barley for quinoa. I did not use any salt but had some on the table; no one added any because the cheddar made it salty enough. Do like the idea of feta, maybe next time. And there will be a next time!

I love every ingredient in this recipe, and yet there’s something missing… I’ve been eating leftovers and trying to tweak the dish a little and just can’t make it work. I followed the instructions and the broccoli was perfect raw. I won’t make this again.
I plan to make this with jicama instead of the apple. It sounds delicious!
Delicious! I’m not sure I see this as a meal, but I’ll definitely make it again for picnics or potlucks. It’d fit well next to coleslaw, potato or pasta salads on a buffet.

This was very tasty.  The broccoli does soften after it's been in the dressing for awhile -- but be sure to chop it finely.   It also makes a TON of salad, so good for lunches all week.

Make sure you have plenty of dental floss handy afterwards!
We used kerry gold cheese. This is good!
Instead of a whole head of broccoli I used two bunches worth of broccoli stems I had saved up—spiralizing the main stem and chopping the smaller parts. Definitely saving this one.

We loved this salad. Ate it alongside bowls of creamy pinto beans with cheddar and scallions stirred in. Great combo.Increased dressing by about a quarter and it seemed just right. Fine to juice whole lemon. May cut quinoa  to 3/4 cup next time. Broccoli was not too crunchy. Can’t wait to try with cashews and some diced raw zucchini, or roasted winter squash cubes. Dried apricots or golden raisins would work fine instead of cranberries. Yum !

Kicking myself for not reading the comments - waaaaau too much quinoa- going to get more broccoli - I imagine this is amazing as I do broccoli salads and that dressing was great- looking forward to the second try lol
I concur with other reviewers in preferring a higher vinaigrette-to-broccoli ratio than called for in the recipe.
I had to double the dressing.

I found this recipe to be like a kitchen sink cookie, or a recipe to entice a child to eat his vegetables, so laden is it with ingredients that mask the flavors of the main ingredients.  It is an inundation.  My husband was drawn to it because it features quinoa and broccoli.  Little did either of us realize the Inordinate qualities of cheese, pecans, and mustard, among other ingredients, that were called for.  It had a good flavor. Nevertheless, we won't be making it again.
.

No, just no. I’m a huge fan of grain salads, and I love all the other ingredients, but this just didn’t come together in any meaningful way for me. The textures were weird, from mushy quinoa to crunchy broccoli and the dressing did nothing to amalgamate the flavors. In all these years of cooking NYTimes recipes this is the first one I won’t make again.
It's delicious just as it is, but added some onions were a good addition.
This salad is delicious. I made it as per the recipe with the exception of the nuts. The only change I would make is with the portions. As a side dish it serves much more than 4-6.
This salad is addictive, an no, you don't have to blanch the broccoli!  I added chopped kale (had some I needed to use up) to the dressing to tame it before combining everything else together.

We made this for lunches this week, I added chicken jalapeño sausage for more protein and it was absolutely delicious. I added the whole lemon instead of half as well! If you are meal prepping this for lunches, I would recommend adding the pecans morning of as they lose a little bit of their crunch sitting in the quinoa. Will definitely make again!

Yes, raw broccoli is not appealing. Cheddar cheese in a salad? No. As I'm making this salad, I questioned WHY would I cook something I know I'm not going to like. Thankfully I took the leap of faith and prepared it as is. I did not parboil the broccoli as suggested by others, and against my better judgement I added the cheddar. Ya'll... it turned out so good. Quick, easy, and delicious. I finely chopped the broccoli and used grated Kerrygold cheese. It really only took 30 minutes. Try it.

Delish! I think next time I’d use less honey as I found it a pinch sweet. Maybe will try with feta instead of cheddar or at least very very sharp cheddar as the type I got blended a little more than I would want. I was skeptical but agree that the raw broccoli is fantastic in every way. Would be curious to add a protein, maybe even cured?

I had leftover steamed broccoli so I looked for recipe ideas and found this yummy salad. Followed the recipe except the cheese. I always have crumbled Gorgonzola blue cheese on hand so I used a handful of that. The tangy flavor of the cheese is a wonderful contrast to the sweetness of the cranberries. The dressing is very good and I plan on making it for any salad!

If you're not going to eat the salad right away, I recommend reserving the cheddar cheese and adding it at the last minute. I've found that after several hours, the flavor of cheese leaches out, and the cheese loses its texture if left in a moist salad.

UPDATE to my original post: flavors blended nicely overnight. Overall, salad was a bit dry so I made a second batch of dressing and used the whole thing - much tangier and tastier! Great foil to a rich entree. This would be a great salad to bring to a party or potluck or just please the folks at home. Recommend making this in advance.

This recipe is very good BUT: it makes an enormous amount of salad, suitable for a party but not for everyday; rec halving the ingredients but keeping the amount of dressing THE SAME.  Also: use a food processor!  Grate the cheese instead of chopping it.
My husband made quick work of this salad, a swimmer and a hiker, he has a hearty appetite!  Did not use the lemon, overkill!

I’ve made several times now. I don’t always add cheese. Sometimes I’ve used substituted cheese and nut types. Very delicious for a quick lunch!
I’ll use a large broccoli bunch and cut as small as I can. The dressing softens the broccoli and is good texture. Just leave out the very stiff dried out bottom part of the large stem. Definitely use the smaller stems!

Made it just as directed, the raw broccoli is not an issue, the secret seems to be to chop small.  I think cooking it would have made it mushy.   Very wonderful blend of flavors...BUT, it makes a ton!  Way more than I expected, could have halved it.

Husband couldn't believe the broccoli was raw. For his heart health, no cheese, less oil and fewer pecans. Still extremely enjoyable. Like did others, I used less quinoa and a few extra lemon squeezes.
Strongly recommend halving the honey :)

Made this following the recipe with raw broccoli as per other comments. I used only 3oz cheddar which was more than enough for me and substituted dried cherries for cranberries. Will add more apple next time for better sweetness balance. Lots of prep time but pretty tasty!

We enjoyed this and will make it again. Instead of cheddar, I added bleu cheese at the time of serving, I used raisins instead of cranberries and and toasted walnuts instead of pecans. Add all of the lemon juice. It kept well over a few days.

I love this salad! It’s easy enough for ME to make successfully (this means it’s easy enough for you to make, no matter your skill level)! It’s a great meal prep lunch recipe and the mix of textures and zippy flavor mean you won’t get tired of it over several days! It’s perfect as written and I highly recommend.
Add more apple and more (double?)the vinaigrette- for vegans, substitute agave (or agave/ maple) for the honey. Swap out vegan cheddar or similar vegan cheese for the cheddar.
Step 1 is cook the quinoa. Clearly 1 cup of uncooked quinoa.

I just made this for the first time and almost forgot to add the quinoa, because it looked so beautiful and whole!

Trying it with half the quinoa and will adjust.
Oddly, there was no broccoli available today. We subbed broccolini and consider the result to be excellent. We’ll do this one again!

I had a very juicy lemon, and I still added the juice from both halves (not just one).  It needed salt and pepper to finish, and I think I'll try making a greater quantity of dressing for next time.  The amount of dressing was "light", but good.  I just like a little bit more. 
 Also, instead of dried cranberries (too sweet, I think), I used dried Montmorency cherries.  Loved the sweet-tart bite of those.  Overall, very tasty and definitely a winner.  We will be making this again.

I do a sales like this with shaved raw brussels sprouts.
Also works well.

This is incredibly easy and so delicious. I made exactly as written. Had to sub golden raisins and pine nuts as that’s what I had on hand. Next time I’ll double it as I almost ate the entire bowl by myself. Thanks NYT!
When I make this — and I will — I’ll substitute crumbled Gorgonzola or some other blue cheese for the cheddar.
This salad is delicious. I'lll be making this salad a lot.  I did make an additional third of the dressing because I needed a bit more for my taste. Highly recommend it!
Substituted pumpkin seeds for the nuts since I'm allergic to nuts and worked great.  About 1/2 cup.

I was skeptical at first about the raw broccoli but a comment here made me more adventurous and I did make this salad, but without the quinoa or any added grain.  Adding the grain seemed to elevate the salad into more of a meal (esp. if one adds some cooked chicken?) but I could imagine that some cooked barley would well and be more substantive than quinoa.  No, I did not parboil.  The vinaigrette did the trick to "cook" the broccoli as in a ceviche.

Ok, so for this boomer, is "rinsing" and "straining" a joke?  I wondered about it before I tried since my experience w/quinoa is that neither of those words work well with the grain.  I parboiled the brocco and it was fine though I will def try this as designed and prob w/an orzo or brown rice.  Unless of course the water actually does drain from the quinoa before the broc goes bad.
OMGoodness.  I pulled the jar of CHIA instead of Quinoa!  No WONDER it was such a disaster.  Got to laugh at our crazy kitchen mistakes. oh well, it still tastes ok

Loved this flavorful salad.  Served it as my grain and vegetable. I steamed the broccoli, per the family preference.  I was out of pecans and dried cranberries so substituted almonds and raisins.  The “sauce” was delicious and really pulled all the flavors together.  Next time I will still steam the broccoli and explore completing the recipe as written.

Used barley instead of quinoa as I prefer the chewiness and nutty flavor.  Grated cheddar because I didn't read the recipe closely enough.  Absolutely delicious!  Makes a lot.  Serves 4 lumberjacks, maybe.  8 lunch main dish servings for most adults and easily feeds 12 as a side.
Loved this.  I couldn’t find cranberries at any store, so I used  dried cherries.  I also forgot to add the Dijon mustard, still was wonderful!  I will definitely make again!
Cheddar, ugh. Better: Feta, blue(or bleu), quality parmesan, even mozz.
Did not include the mustard, seems counter-intuitive, was delicious without.
I made this, per directions, and it was delicious .   In the encore version, I added diced celery and pomegranate seeds, resulting in a bowl of glistening salad gems.
There is no need to have cheddar or some kind if cheese in every vegetable dish. The sauces and dressings will suffice.
Delicious refreshing salad.  Followed the recipe exactly.  Lasted 3 days in fridge -- tasty to the very end.

This is so good and easy to make. Got me out of my cooking slump.  Filling with yummy flavor and healthy ingredients.  I did not have honey so I substituted maple syrup. I also blanched the broccoli briefly.  Will make again. And not just for Mediterranean Week.

I made this today and really love the flavors…but I think the recipe should be a little clearer with quantity of the quinoa. I used 1 cup of uncooked quinoa because it said how to cook the quinoa in the recipe but that is way too much when you mix it all together.
Swapped sunflower seeds for nuts due to allergy, and it was great.

I made this exactly as written other than I was using up some holiday spiced pecans that had been toasted with some sugar and spices, rather than plain nuts.  I was looking for a way to use up some cooked quinoa. I didn't chop the broccoli, I tore it into little florets and left out the stems. It was better than I expected. Something about the little bites of cheddar really makes it -- there's  sweetness, savoriness, creaminess and crunch. Make it, especially if you have leftover quinoa!

Yum!! I ended up doubling the dressing for the amount of broccoli and quinoa and was not unhappy that I did. Re: raw broccoli - try it! It doesn't taste raw once macerated in the dressing and retains that really nice crunch. And yes on a good aged cheddar. Levels everything up.
I really enjoyed this salad. Next time I would double the lemon.

I made this for a potluck where the request was to keep foods low sodium and plant-based. So I added only very little salt and replaced the cheddar with nutritional yeast. Despite these changes (especially the next to no amount of salt) it tasted quite good. I'm quite impressed with this one. Plus it's a pretty dish!

This is wonderful!  The dressing is fabulous, the broccoli is perfect just as the recipe is written.  I can envision lots of substitutions, but for the first try as written it was excellent.  Colorful, fresh tasting, healthy!
Tasty! I wanted about 25 percent more dressing. Saved cheese, nuts and cranberries aside to add over the days I ate this.

I made this with 2x the lemon, half the honey and no apple. I think that addressed the issue with sweetness as I found it to be well-balanced and tasty. Agree with the comments regarding the number of servings this recipe makes - it will be difficult for this small household to finish this off within 3 days. I would make it again but would definitely cut the recipe in half.

Try this salad, it’s delicious. I didn’t have pecans so I used slivered almonds and sunflower seeds and it was still very good, although I think pecans would be amazing. I would not have tried this due to the raw broccoli but thanks to the comments, I’m glad I did. The finely chopped broccoli is tamed by the dressing.
Just made this - omitted the cheddar since it’s a side dish with salmon - and it’s definitely a keeper!

Delicious!

A few modifications:
- I doubled the dressing and measured with my heart when dressing the broccoli. Definitely recommend making 2x or 3x.
- The dressing strikes me as quite sweet and quite tangy. The acid is great once everything is dressed, but there are many other sweet elements in the salad, so I'd cut the honey by 1/2 or 2/3.
- I added maybe 1/3 of a finely chopped red onion, rinsed well under cold water to remove the bite. The savory note from an allium is a must.
I'm a big fan of this recipe! The sharp cheddar was such a great addition. This kept perfectly in the fridge all week to have for lunch. Great way to get in lots of micronutrients!

Made this as written except halved the honey and left out the cranberries after deciding it was sweet enough with the apple.  The raw broccoli, like so many have said, is excellent.  Overall, however, this salad to me is “meh”.  I think it’s missing a bitter element to offset the sweetness.

I used farro instead of quinoa.  Added an additional lemon’s worth of juice.  This dish was outstanding.  I plain toasted my pecans and they were outstanding.  This was dinner and I couldn’t have been happier.

Made this tonight.  We used a pouch of President's Choice microwave quinoa, a package of shredded cheddar (vs chopping it up ourselves) and Craisins.  My husband chopped the broccoli & apple, and next time, I would probably chop it into smaller pieces than he did. The dressing is very tasty, and I almost wish there had been a little more of it. We both very much enjoyed it and will look forward to eating up the leftovers for lunch over the next day or two.
This recipe gave me mushy quinoa. Will not be using again.
Followed the recipe as is - letting it sit for a few hours so the dressing saturates the broccoli made it even better. I will make this agin.

I usually spend a little time on Sunday making lunches for the week and this one was great!  It kept really well, was easy to eat at my desk and has both carbs and veggies.  (I did leave out the cheese because I didn't have any and the apple because I thought the salad would keep better without.)  I paired it with a protein and had well balanced and delicious lunches all week.  I did not par boil the broccoli but it tasted mellow and cooked with the vinegar.  Def in the meal prep rotation!
Delicious recipe! I didn’t have apple so I substituted pear and didn’t do cheddar bc that sounded weird. Saving this for the future!

I’ve made this twice now, and its on the menu again for tomorrow. No need to blanch the broccoli—really! And Im not at all a fan of raw broccoli but the dressing takes care of that. I only use half the cheese for health reason but I make sure it is really high quality sharp cheddar. Serving with roasted chicken breasts (the ATK recipe).

I love this recipe. I upped the amount of nuts and somewhat the cheese. 
My favorite way to have this is to soft poach two eggs by cracking in a small ramekin and putting in microwave for one minute and putting on top of salad and mixing it together. So satisfying and a great breakfast.
I have also mashed avacado in it and it was delish.

I made this exactly as written- unusual for me. I did grate the cheese. My daughter and I liked it. Tastes healthy and a bit different. I’d like to try it with a different heartier grain. It does make a lot of salad!

This was excellent. I made it as directed - as others have noted, the broccoli didn’t taste raw after sitting in the dressing. For anyone who doesn’t have ALL the ingredients, I tasted it with just the broccoli, quinoa, and dressing and it was fabulous. The apple, cheddar and pecans are great but not necessary.

I tried this dish tonight…my observations - while I thought it was tasty, it needed a bit more dressing. So, added the other lemon half juiced and a bit more extra virgin olive oil…still thought the flavor profile was amiss. While looking through the frig produce drawer saw fresh green onions - sliced a couple up - it added a much needed zip.
I left out the cheddar & it needs something. I think I’ll try adding feta. I also used loquat fruit since I had some I needed to use. I’ll make this again.
I used Granny Smith apple.
I love this salad as written, although I left out the cheese cubes as a personal preference to eat less cheese. So much texture and flavor! Thank you.
Delicious and easy to make. Followed recipe as written except 1/2 ingredients for only the two of us. Great for summer vegetarian guests.
Double the dressing for sure — raw broccoli is not pretty enough be doing all that without enough dressing! Really good with a lot of texture though.

I didn't have the cheese, or cranberries on hand but wanted to make this anyway.  I substituted raisins, and it was delicious!  Not surprised tho, I have loved all of Sohla's recipes that I've tried.    Thanks for another winner Sohla!

Great make ahead for a dinner party salad.  The video was helpful to see how she cut the broccoli.  I was skeptical about the cheddar, but it works.  I really love this salad and will enjoy mixing it up with different grains, nuts, and cheeses.

I wanted to keep this for a few days so I didn’t add the cheddar or nuts until I was eating it, when I added them directly to my serving. I think I’ll try with dates instead of cranberries next time, and maybe feta instead of cheddar.

"Aggressive" salting amounted to 2 tsps. for me, hardly the fistful I've seen Sohia throw in on videos. It was WAAAAAY too much for us, but I was able to temper it with extra lemon. I used goat cheese and toasted walnuts, subbed fresh corn for apples b/c that's what I had on hand, added red onions. Other than the saltiness, which I'm tasting as I type, it was delish.
Wow, this is surprisingly good. Planning to use as an easy and relatively healthy lunch all week. Added a can of chickpeas for a little extra protein but I suspect diced chicken would be nice too.

Really delicious salad, and if you’re a bit iffy about raw broccoli this is the one to get you over that fear. I say that having still blanched the broccoli for about 10 seconds beforehand and ran under ice water, but it was still crunchy and lovely. Requires more salt than you think overall and do take note on the emphasis of heavily seasoning your water for the quinoa.
My go-to take along, although all the chopping always makes it take longer to make than I think it is going to.  Leisurely day ahead with a good podcast  to make the time pass.

Subbed pistachios for pecans
Could have doubled dressing
Needs a bit more dressing, so cut back the broccoli-quinoa to offset.

Be sure to follow the steps in order. Apples need to go in with broccoli to get coated with acid so they don't brown. I didn't do that, my apples are now brown, but I will next time. For my family's tastes, subbed pistachios and goat cheese. After putting it all together, think it may need a LITTLE more dressing. I made another half batch to add to taste or to drizzle over protein to make a main course. Super yummy!
Delicious.  The farro I subbed for quinoa matched the heartiness of the other ingredients well.  I added a chopped HB egg for some protein which turned out to add a nice soft texture.

I was looking for a dinner that didn’t heat up the house on a very hot day and this raw broccoli salad was perfect. Quinoa cooks fast. I was tempted not to roast the pecans but, as you know, it is always worth it. Yum.

Super salad! I didn’t have any quinoa on hand so I used couscous. I also likely chopped broccoli too much (recipe says a fine chop) because I didn’t look at the posted pic, but it still tastes delicious. I agree with others who wrote that it is best to not cook the broccoli. The dressing really makes this salad pop! This is a keeper for sure.

Wish I would have added half of the quinoa. It calls for 1 cup, rinsed. But maybe I misunderstood and was supposed to measure a cup after it was cooked? Don't get me wrong, it's delicious and I like quinoa so no harm done- however it gobbled up all of the ingredients and now it just looks like quinoa is the main ingredient. Bummed. It's a HUGE batch for me and my husband, but we will eat all of it.

Made this with two small changes - left out the apple (didn’t have one) and only used half the honey. It is so delicious. I cook the quinoa ahead of time, making a big batch in the Instant Pot and then freezing one cup portions for future use. Will definitely make again!

This is a perfect salad. I too was scared of the raw broccoli but the acid "cooks" it - it's super delicious this way! 
Made just as listed but second time I went a little heavy on the lemon and a lil extra cheese and nuts cause those are the fun bits. Super filling, delicious and makes really great cold lefotvers.

Great!  Checks the box for “eat more vegetables”.
- let just-purchased broccoli macerate - imho the broccoli and the Dijon vinaigrette are the only mandatory ingredients, the rest can be free-form
- subbed Asian pear for apple
- subbed toasted cashews and pepitas for pecans (toasted sunflower seeds would be great)
- subbed goji berries for cranberries
- still undecided about cheese to use … thinking about it while I finish my bowl of this salad. :)
- subbed leftover brown rice quinoa mix for quinoa
It needed more dressing. I would double it next time, but the flavor combination was fantastic. It's a great alternative to the recipe with mayo!
I used the whole lemon in the dressing, and substituted walnuts or almonds if I didn’t have pecans.

It was very good.  I served chicken on top for the non-vegetarians, but like someone else’s idea of adding garbanzo beans for next time.  I will skip the cheese next time - it didn’t seem to add much and it looked odd to have the yellow cheese in the salad.  I used raisins because it was what I had, but I look forward to going with cranberries next time.

This is very good, although I changed a few things to prevent a trip to the grocery store. Made the dressing as written (in the amount suggested), but subbed pine nuts for the pecans, feta for the cheddar, and dried currants for the cranberries. Also didn't have an apple. As long as you get the crunchy, salty, and sweet components, this will stay balanced. Did the quinoa hands-off in the rice cooker earlier in the day - the rest came together in less than 15 minutes.
Makes many more side servings! Don't double
It was lovely - I substituted farro
I followed the recipe as written - I discovered I didn't have apple cider vinegar but I did have a bottle of boiled apple cider.  I think it was an excellent substitute.
I used one tbsp of hot honey to give it a little kick. Was perfect.

Do not rinse the quinoa.
Cook it in just over 2x the water, on slow simmer, covered, until just barely all the water is absorbed. Turn onto a cookie sheet to cool slightly and steam off. Beautiful perfect little beads with halos. Nutty and al dente chewy.
What a surprise! Made exactly as directed - such an unusual combination of ingredients. Very easy to eat. The raw broccoli is absolutely not a problem.

I agree with other comments that it needs to be clear that the broccoli should macerate in the dressing. I also think the dressing quantity was not enough to cover the amount of broccoli requested or things in the salad. In the future, I would quickly steam the broccoli to give it a bright brain color and then macerate for at least 15 minutes before mixing with the other ingredients.

Followed the recipe almost exactly, except subbed raisins for the cranberries, because I don't like the refined sugar that is added to most dried cranberries.  Was tempted to par-boil the broccoli but so glad I did not.
My children, who do not like quinoa, ate this happily. My husband, who does not like vegetarian food, ate this happily. What a winner

This tasted great with brown rice instead of quinoa, and maple syrup instead of honey. I had some sliced avocado with it.
Next time I’ll make some of Yotam Ottolenghi’s quick pickled sumac onions to add a bit more sharpness to the flavour.

@AJR
Ooh. Yes. Pickled red onions! Thanks for the idea.
This is my favorite recipe EVER!!! Makes me love love broccoli and so simple. Tonight I had it over some rigatoni for a full meal. Thank you nyt

Very tasty.  Great base recipe to tweak to your own tastes, and a healthier dupe to the mayo based “broccoli bacon salad” that is so addictive.  Our tips: Double the dressing recipe, sub in some hot honey, needs aggressive salt/pep seasoning (as do most grain salads for us).  Leave broccoli raw, no need to blanch.

I agree with many of the reviewers of this recipe, that some of the ingredients are questionable depending on your taste.  I revised the recipe to include:  white quinoa, feta cheese, pine nuts, par-boiled broccoli mostly crowns w/stems chopped medium, 3 T chopped chives, green tart apple chopped not fine.  Dressing:  3 T olive oil,2 T sherry vinegar, one clove crushed garlic,  and pepper shaken in jar.  Add to room temp quinoa and then add other ingredients w/chives and toasted pine nuts last.

Made this a crucifer salad with broccoli, cauliflower and cabbage. No cranberries so used dried blueberries and chopped up apricots. Mix of nuts: pecans, walnuts and pistachios. Used about 50 g of quinoa; 60 would have been better. Make all the dressing for 2/3 of the recipe. Very tasty.
Extremely weird salad, somehow tasted pretty good regardless.
It's delicious have made it many times.  Would not change any part of the recipe.
I'm curious if anyone has suggestions for a non-dairy cheese that might go with this or another way to add an additional protein for someone who is vegan. Thanks so much!

@Brett quinoa and pecans are already high in protein so you can just leave the cheddar out. Or maybe add beans. Some people will suggest vegan cheese —I’m not fond of it personally, and it rarely has much protein or other nutritional value. Be aware that most vegans don’t eat honey so replace with something like maple or agave syrup.
I can only assume that Sohla El-Waylly tested this recipe to the ends of the earth because it is PERFECT!

Subbed Trader Joe’s farro for the quinoa (had on hand), bit extra cheese, whole lemon's worth of juice, otherwise as written. So tasty and easy. Chop the broccoli into small pieces, no need to cook it first!
Have made this twice, 2nd time without the quinoa and it's excellent either way.

I just made this, and it’s pretty simple and tasty, a lot like the tabouli at one of my favorite restaurants. The raw broccoli when chopped fine lends a fresh coleslaw-like texture. It has a strong tart/sour zing freshly made, though it may mellow after sitting in the fridge. I couldn’t really taste the cheddar cheese and may try it with crumbled feta next time, or just skip the cheese all together. I would consider keeping this on repeat in the summer when it’s too hot to want to cook.
Very tasty!  But, as a side dish, way too much food food for a family of 3 or 4.  Cut it in half.   For me, the cheddar is unnecessary.

I made this with substitutes based on what I had on hand. Basmati brown rice instead of quinoa, an feta instead if cheddar. Everything else was according to the recipe and delicious. Served it with roast chicken for a warmish early spring dinner.
Also, lightly steamed the broccoli and ran under cold water to make is just slightly tender.
Great recipe. However don't forget to rinse the quinoa, as it says, or it will be too salty.
The cheddar made this healthy dish just sing!  Made exactly to recipe.  Yum.

This makes a HUGE amount of salad (almost 14 cups worth) - great for a week and a half worth of generous lunches for 1, or a potluck. I made as written for my husband and I (delicious!) and another time with farro for a work lunch potluck (it disappeared!). It’s now in the regular rotation for plan-ahead lunches, though I half the recipe.
I cut the amount of quinoa in half and added some red onion. It is delicious and will definitely make again!

This is very good.  I have made it several times. The last time I made more dressing, maybe 1/2 or 1/3 more and upped the lemon and vinegar.  I only used half of  the quinoa and after it was cooked and cooled, put it in a bowl and poured half of the dressing on it and stirred well. I dressed the rest of the ingredients with the other half of the dressing and then combined the two.  It was Excellent.

Raw broccoli chopped up with toppings is a completely different experience than eating a stalk from a raw veggie platter. Highly recommend trying it. You might get hooked (like me) and find it’s a healthy time saver for a variety of salad options all week long.
Great to make ahead of time for camping or to otherwise feed a crowd. Easy to serve the cheese and nuts on the side to accommodate allergies.
Maybe my quinoa was a little old? It just wasn’t that great for me and it seemed like a lot of work chopping up fruits and veggies for something that didn’t wow me.

Make this about twice a month for a long time now! It’s great to have in the fridge when too tired to make lunch, or as a packed lunch for the family.

It’s a great way to get in veggies and healthy grains and my husband who had never had quinoa before, loves it!
Just follow the recipe! It’s so good! Make sure to chop the broccoli very finely and you will have a fantastic lunch.

I’ve made it twice now- exactly as written but with goat cheddar instead of bovine.  
Rave reviews- took it for a neighborhood picnic where it was devoured! 
Great recipe- for again and again. 
And yes, broccoli softens in the mix- no need to precook in any way

IMHO, this was a big Meh. I used 1lb of raw brocc and 1/2 cup quinoa, but the dressing was inadequate. Lemon zest was excessive. Apple and nuts were nice. Sharp cheddar? No. 

I "fixed" it by adding mayo (~1/3 c) and half a chopped red onion. Gorgonzola was delish; feta would be good. 

But I'm never making this again. Gonna stick with the classic Midwestern (NOT Southern) broccoli salad: mayo/sour cream/ACV dressing, chopped celery, dried cranberries, red onion, & sunflower seeds.

This recipe is lovely. I only had candied pecans, so I omitted the cranberries. I used a granny smith apple and left out the cheese (partner is dairy free). served along with rotisserie chicken for an easy weeknight meal.

I LOVE this salad. Totally addictive. Was a little sweet for my palate. Subbed goji berries for cran (cuz they have no added sugar). Reduced honey to 1 tablespoon. Omitted the apple and cheddar actually. Added some chives, parsley, and shredded red cabbage for more punch and crunch. Took this camping and it just got better and better over several days.
@Lisa S I agree. I doubled checked that I did everything correctly because I couldn't understand all the 5 * reviews?!?
This salad has entered into my meal prep routine on a regular basis. I save time chopping and use my food processors slicing tool for the broccoli and the apple and it makes a huge difference!!

Delicious.  Can be made in advance which is always nice for the host.  Excellent with chicken or pork.  1. If you finely chop the broccoli and apple the salad is not as pretty as it would be if you chop according to the recipe photo.  2.  Adding extra dressing eliminates the need for added salt and pepper and lifts the flavor.  3. This recipe can only be made in 30 minutes if there are 2 cooks in the kitchen.  4.  Servings are generous.

So good! I made the amount of dressing called for, then did another half batch of dressing, then ended up doing yet another half batch. Next time I make, I plan on doubling dressing from the beginning. Secondly, I am glad I did not parboil the broccoli. Lastly... I'm the kind of person who doesn't necessarily care about adding cheese to a salad. But with this recipe, it made a big difference (in the best way)!!
This is delicious. Made one sub:  salted,roasted pepitas for walnuts. Keeps well. Half the recipe and use full amount of dressing— unless you’re feeding a crowd.

excellent.  don't be miserly with the salt.  Might be able to add chopped scallions. bagged shredded cheddar saved time but the time to chop to small cubes would be worth it.  bagged broccoli florets probably a time saver--I still chopped them a bit, and had to trim off a lot of dried stem ends.

I made this the night before for a picnic for four; put in quart baggies w/names, so each of us could take the leftovers home for another meal if we liked it - was a hit!  I thought it was a bit dry, so doubled the dressing, added half to the salad to macerate overnight.  Brought the remaining bit to the picnic; only one of us needed to add extra.  Leftovers were happily taken home by all!  This works because the broccoli is diced; no need to parboil. Will try farro next time!

The combination of flavors and textures in this salad is wonderful. Next time, I'll make two small changes: make the quinoa the night before to eliminate wetness and add the nuts to each serving right before eating to prevent soggy nuts. Marinating the broccoli in the dressing takes away the rawness, making cooking it unnecessary.

Hello all you beautiful people ! 
This is a delicious, bright, healthy salad, and keeps really well for a few days.
A little bit fussy, so not sure if we’ll do it again.

I’ve made this many times and prefer to use farro over quinoa. I usually skip the cranberries (stuck in teeth!) and find it’s still tasty without. Use whatever nuts I have in the pantry. Pecans are great
Delicious!! Enough salad for an army, or at least a platoon. We served it as a side to 6 people and had lots left over. Since it easily could be cut in half, adjust your recipe accordingly.

This is on heavy rotation at my house!  I replace with dried cherries, pistachios, and aged, crumbly cheddar, because that's what we keep on hand. I can eat the entire bowl in one sitting. Better the next day -- gives the raw broccoli time to mellow.

This was pretty good and definitely healthy, but the taste is a little bland. I added a little aged white balsamic and it tasted better. Also, like someone else suggested I feel like Feta would be better than Sharp Cheddar as the Cheddar kind of got lost in flavor.

Do NOT parboil the broccoli! If you’re concerned it won’t be tender or flavorful enough, just chop it smaller - into a dice. The commenters who have said that the broccoli “cooks” in the acid of the dressing are 100% correct - soften but remains toothy. This would not be nearly as delicious with cooked broccoli. Made it to the recipe, we devoured it (teenagers included) and ro-sham-bo’d to decide who would get leftovers for lunch tomorrow…

@MsMcK I seriously agree.  Have made and loved this recipe multiple times.  Today, decided to blanch broccoli (45 seconds) as I have found that leftovers are a bit washed out looking the next day.  I already regret my decision…

As the salad marinates, the broccoli releases some water and also “cooks” in the vinaigrette turning a nice green.  I upped the pecans and cheese a bit but it’s excellent as is. I have also made this with a little bit of chipotle in adobo added to the dressing  which adds a touch to Smokey heat.
So the broccoli are raw?
Yes. That's normal for a fresh salad.
Is the cheddar absolutely necessary? I'm strongly considering leaving it out - just doesn't sound like something that would go well with the other ingredients :/

The cheddar is what tipped the balance for me and convinced me to add this to this week's meal list! But of course it isn't 100% necessary--the recipe police won't come to your house and arrest you if you leave it out. Or maybe you could replace it with crumbled feta...that sounds pretty good too.
Cheddar goes well with broccoli in soup so maybe? I agree with the other suggestion about feta, or if you aren't averse to it crumbled blue might be nice.
I don’t care for mustard. Any good substitutes?
Maybe try some light miso and add a little more vinegar and or lemon juice?
Excellent salad. I subbed cooked kamut for the quinoa and doubled the dressing amounts.
If you look closely at the image, they present, the broccoli is not raw
Recipe is pretty good, but what a ridiculous and unnecessary name.

I made this tonight and absolutely loved it. I try all sorts of dressings and this one is a delight. I used a nice crisp tart apple, good sharp cheddar and good quality honey. I also steamed my broccoli a bit because that is how we like it in salads.  And I only had chopped dried cherries but would have used cranberries. You can make this exactly as per the recipe and you will enjoy it greatly I promise. Simple but really nice recipe!

Raw Vs steamed?  I make the Italian Broccoli salad often. The simple oil and vinegar dressing softens the raw broccoli as it sits. I always make a day ahead. Keeps getting better! It calls for provolone cheese. I cannot like cheese with in that salad tried three tries but I add more almonds and pepperonci 
add more almonds and peperoncini. The
Is it one cup of cooked quinoa? Or all the quinoa from one cup uncooked? That’s too much quinoa.
It is one cup of uncooked quinoa. Instructions for cooking the quinoa are in the first paragraph of the recipe.

Made this today, exquisite. I love broccoli, and this is an incredibly tasty way to enjoy it. I did follow one reader's comment and blanched the broccoli ever so slightly, and I preferred it this way. This will become a staple in our kitchen!
The recipe calls for one cup of quinoa, cooked or uncooked. Big difference!
The directions for cooking the quinoa are in the first paragraph of the recipe.

Substituted walnuts for pecans, finely grated pecorino for cubed cheddar, maple syrup for honey.  Didn’t have anything to sub for dried cranberries so left them out. Consider adding chopped fennel stalks next time. Super tasty, will definitely make again and can imagine many variations on this theme. Team raw broccoli and dressing quantity as written!

I prepared this today and am generous giving it four stars.  Of course there were some changes.  Brown rice instead of quinoa, blanched the broccoli, no cheddar which is not my preference, and I added some Aleppo pepper, too.  I found the dish sort of bland/not enough flavor.  Next time, I’d farro which is more toothsome than rice, add more nuts, cranberries, and Aleppo pepper, feta, too.  I wanted to like this as a way to eat more healthy broccoli, but the dish didn’t ring the bell.
This was absolutely delicious as-is (except I didn’t have the dried cranberries and they’d give the sweet piece that I was missing). Five stars! I’ll take it for lunch this week.
Could I use sunflower seeds instead of pecans (need to avoid nuts)?
sure!
don't normally like dried fruit in vegetable salads, but lately see it a lot. How's this salad without the dried cranberries, anyone tried this without the dried cranberries? How was it?
I'm not a big quinoa fan, though I'll give this a try. What might I use instead? Rice seems too mushy and bland.

This recipe is definitely a keeper. I made it on a sub-zero night so blanched the broccoli and added the quinoa before it cooled. Used dried cherries instead of cranberries and chopped ups green onion that needed using. It’s a starch and salad in one bowl! A perfect accompaniment to salmon or chicken, or as a veggie stand alone.

very tasty.  it seems like it would make a lot, so i cut the recipe in half for the first try.  i used 15-year cheddar - the tartness and cheese crystals added a lot, I thought.  next time I will try with toasted walnuts or toasted hazelnuts.  (I have a whole drawer of cheeses so could switch that out, too.  aged swiss?)
I always rinse my quinoa before cooking to eliminate the bitter outer coating. Otherwise this recipe is perfect!
I didn't have quinoa so substituted pearl couscous and still delicious!
This is the best salad I have made in a while. A , would recommend. Also makes a ton so good for leftovers

I inadvertently left out the apple and then added it afterward and preferred it without. Perhaps because I used leftover steamed broccoli instead of raw and subbed sliced almonds for the pecans since that's what I had on hand. It was a terrific lunch and a nice change of pace.

Just made this for dinner after reading the recipe.  Followed directions.  Flavors will meld together as it sits in refrigerator for dinner.   Pairing it with Perfectly Roasted Pork Tenderloin and golden beets.  
Of course, I had to sample it before placing in refrigerator.  It is delicious! Will put in my rotation recipes.
What is the best way to toast the almonds for this recipe?

I toasted the pecans (not almonds) on the stovetop in a small pan on low heat. Works great! Just don't forget about them — stir occasionally, and try to notice when they start to become fragrant. Good luck!
Manchego cheese works if you're out of cheddar! Also added more lemon juice at the end, but otherwise made as written.
Everyone here from the South knows that raw broccoli an a salad like this is delicious. Find it at every church function, birthday party and potluck. Definitely making this version soon!
Cheddar?  I use Feta?
This is a really good salad -- nice mix of textures and flavors. I will happily make and serve this again, as is.
Thought this was very good! I made it pretty much as specified in the recipe and it was delicious.

Made this exactly as directed and it is wonderful! Looking forward to seeing how the leftovers improve with time. Definitely do NOT parboil the broccoli. The raw crunch mellows as it sits but still maintains a nice bite.

raw broccoli is not appealing. I always briefly parboil chopped broccoli then plunge in ice water to stop the cooking if I plan to use it in a salad. Not only does this improve the flavor, it brightens the color to a very pretty and palatable bright green.
Wow, this was great! I subbed pistachios for the pecans and parboiled the broccoli. Otherwise, followed faithfully. A delicious result. Loved.
Made this with bulgur and added a handful of mint. - delicious !

Awesome recipe - a complete meal! Made it as written; had everything on hand except pecans so I subbed walnuts. Like another reviewer, I too used a bit less quinoa; nicely balanced. If you get a fresh head of broccoli and chop it up fairly small, there are no issues with it being raw. It somewhat “cooks” in the delicious dressing. 
A definite keeper. Thanks Melissa!
I roasted the broccoli. It added great depth.

Made this yesterday and I have had three servings already. Added shredded carrots as well and cooked the broccoli for one minute prior to chopping. Next time will make with feta instead of cheddar. Squeezed half a lemon over it the second day. Delicious.
This works well with millet.

This was delicious. I did blanch the broccoli because I was nervous about so much raw broccoli. After reading some of the notes, I will try this again using raw broccoli. I think it would make a great side dish to bring to a party. Can’t wait to share it with friends and family!
Loved this: chopped broccoli VERY fine; had to substitute yoghurt for cheddar. Great recipe.
Calories? Nutritional information?

This was a great weeknight meal, especially when you cook the quinoa in the Inastapot (thank you to the reader who mentioned that option). I followed the recipe but used vegan cheddar shreds and added some chopped smoked turkey to make it more of a meal

I was super dubious about the cheese but it’s amazing. Seemed a bit dry so added the juice of the other half lemon and an extra tbs cider vinegar. Perfect! This is a great meal-prep or lunchbox recipe. 

I did pre-steam the broccoli briefly because of someone’s digestion issues. I think it would be great either way.
I used mostly honey roasted pecans wi the a sugary coating, and some almonds for the rest of the 3/4 c of nuts. So good! And used a mix of dried cherries and cranberries for the dried fruit.

This was very delicious  I followed all directions and only used sliced toasted almonds for pecans.  I will make this often -it would be very convenient to take on picnics. The broccoli is chopped fine and does not need any cooking- it was very tender

I just made this and it is DELICIOUS! Didn't have enough broccoli so added chopped kale, sprouted brown rice (no quinoa on hand) forgot to add the cheese, which is really not needed. Bright,  fresh,  tasty, and good for you! A Keeper Recipe; am printing it out as we speak!
I really liked it, and would definitely add the extra lemon. I had some quick pickled red onions in the fridge, and they were a good addition too.
I swapped out the quinoa for barley, the cranberries for golden raisins, and added a thinly sliced red onion. Also a generous pinch of black pepper! Tastes amazing :)

This was very, very good. However, whole lot of chopping is involved. I used my old faithful Cuisinart to chop the broccoli, apple, and nuts, and it worked out fine. I also used couscous because I did not have quinoa. The salad was delicious and perfect for a buffet. I will certainly make again.

Dressing is yummy, but there’s not enough of it. This is a huge salad, and I could have used at least 50% more dressing. As is, the salad ends up tasting bland because it’s under-dressed. Adding more lemon helped, but not enough.
Had high hopes for this and it was so easy to make, but it just tasted bad. Didn’t think it tasted good the citrus and quinoa was just not it.

What a refreshing salad on a cold winter day. I made mostly as directed, added the extra half of lemon directly to the vinaigrette. I subbed dried cherries and pistachios because that’s what I had. Will definitely make this again.

I made this today - but only 1/2 recipe as I live alone.  The only substitution I made was using chopped dried apricots for the dried cranberries.  I do like raw broccoli, but with all of the other ingredients and the dressing you might enjoy it even if you don't.  I had a small bowl this afternoon and thought it was really good!  And I bet it will be even better tomorrow.
I wanted to like this salad. There are too many ingredients that do not go well together, why the cheese? I won’t make this again.
Didn’t like it.  A little too mustardy  All the ingredients were so small their taste blended to be tasteless.
Loved it. Do not cook the broccoli! The small pieces, along with the apples give a nice crunch to the salad.

This recipe (sans the cheddar) seems a bit like a wonderful salad the Fairway Market on 74th Street used to sell. It was very popular, but unfortunately  they discontinued it a few months ago.

Although I love fresh veggies, broccoli is one vegetable that I sometimes prefer frozen, particularly when roasting. However this recipe seems like it requires raw broccoli.

Chopping the broccoli and adding the dressing takes away the raw flavor. Needed additional acid, so I added a couple glugs of rice vinegar.  Still a bit bland, so I topped the salad with crumbled feta.  Lasted a couple days in the fridge.  This recipe is a great base and highly adaptable to tastes and ingredients on hand.
made just as directed - parboiled the broccoli, (barely) the second time and it made it even better!
Absolutely delicious.  I added more salt and pepper and lemon juice
This salad makes me want to eat quinoa again. Even my husband wants to eat quinoa. Found the salad better without the cheese. The flavour of the dressing is enough and is excellent.

I also blanched the broccoli as I don’t like it raw despite the vinaigrette. I had cooked quinoa and added two cups as well as a drained and rinsed can of white beans for a bit more protein-y substance. Less honey too. Totally delicious for lunch.
I made the full recipe for myself and ate it for lunch all week (added shredded chicken for some extra protein). It just got better every day! Will definitely keep this in the rotation.
It is better the next day.

This is fine as it goes but I was disappointed to find it didn't come together into a complex whole but was rather just a bowl full of bits of this and that.  Marginally better on the second day.  Perhaps adding quite a lot of grated cheddar would pull it all together.
Cut the recipe as we are just two. Delicious, a hit with hubby, who said "This is great. Please make it again."!!

I was very happy to discover that I already had almost all ingredients. It was too cold to go to the store for the cranberries though. It was really yummy even without them. Great way to eat some raw veggie.
Very good. I added bacon the 2nd day. I’ll make it again.
I leave the pecans until serving time as I like my nuts crunchy, not soggy.
Awesome vegetarian entree or side dish.  I steamed the broccoli before chopping in food processor.  This makes a LOT.  would be great to bring to a pot luck.  This recipe is a keeper!
very good

Delicious! Definitely gets zestier in the refrigerator overnight. Versatile for those who choose not to add all the toppings. Got rave reviews and will for sure make it again! Thank you for this healthy option.

I have had other similar salads that I have enjoyed but unfortunately enjoyment was not to be had with this one. I think it was probably the dressing that was to blame. Or maybe the raw broccoli. My husband did not finish the small serving I put on his plate, and though I ate my larger serving, I wish I hadn’t. All of the salad ingredients make sense but maybe just another dressing and lightly steam the broccoli? Gave me gurgly stomach all night.
This was excellent. I used farro in place of quinoa and it was great. Other than that, I wouldn't change anything.
Loved this! I cut the cheese into pea size squares, and it was plenty. Very delicious, it makes a lot, but I am sure we will eat it all by tomorrow.

I cut recipe in half and made as directed. After eating a sering there is enough for 2-3 more. Delicious. Dressing has a tangy and sweetness about it, No need to parboil the brocolli. Will add more cheddar to the next servings. An easy recipe and so delightful!

I had to substitute wild rice due to my quinoa sensitivity - this was fantastic! Perfect as a summer side. I did take one reviewers suggestion to blanche the broccoli, and I loved how bright it looked. My family loved this, and I plan to make it again!
Is this good the next day?  Was thinking of making it ahead-- would it be good cold?
This is delicious just the way it is!

Loved the recipe although I had less broccoli on hand so my final mixture was more quinoa based. I would suggest adding fresh parsley to the recipe to give it a bit more freshness and double the dressing recipe. Its terrific and you'll want more of it.
made with wheat berries as a sub for quinoa.  Delicious.  A perfect dish to take to a party/dinner/BBQ as you can easily make this a day ahead.  (DO NOT BLANCHE THE BROCCOLI)
Followed the recipe exactly. Does not have the 'raw brocolli' taste if the salad is mixed fully with the dressing. No complaints, it's a good salad!

I made half the recipe. Even without the cheese ( don’t eat) and dried cranberries ( don’t have), it was huge - enough for 8 or 10 servings. I don’t think it can be consumed in 3 days! As for the taste, it didn’t knock my socks off but I hope it will improve with a little marinating time.

This was fabulous.  I added two preserved lemons and a tablespoon of chili crisp.  In my enthusiasm, I accidentally over salted the salad (likely due to the preserved lemons), so I balanced it with two tablespoons of sugar.  We preferred the slightly sweeter version.  I will make this often but will change the cheese.  Cheddar didn't seem quite right, so I think I'll try a mild feta or queso fresco next time.  Do not parboil as others have suggested.
Can't tell you how delicious this is!  Made it with farro because I was out of quinoa, and it is delicious and keeps very well.
I followed the recipe as written.  Delicious!  Salad kept for three days and was a hit with the family.  Perfect for a summer picnic or cold winter day like today.
Is the 1 C quinoa measure for the dried quinoa before cooking; or 1C cooked quinoa. A bit unclear.
This is one cup uncooked quinoa.  You can cook it according to the package instructions, let it cool, then add the cooked quinoa to the broccoli mixture.

This was a delicious salad. Fast, easy, and the mix of tart with sweet and nuts was perfect. Followed the recipe with no diversions.  The acids worked great on raw broccoli. Soo happy with this recipe. Do I have to share? I’m
Eating it as I wait for my chicken to cook… it might be gone before that’s finished!

Very easy and fresh weeknight meal. Agree that you can take or leave the cheese. I didn’t have pecans so I threw in some toasted pepita seeds for texture. Really bright, healthy, and fresh. Would definitely make again.

I made this with farro instead of quinoa because I had it on hand.  Higher fiber content and chewier grain.  Also used 1 & 1/2 TBSP of honey because that's all I had left!  SO GOOD!  Hearty, tasty, filling and healthy entree salad!

This is a great salad. I doubled the dressing because it calls for a lot of quinoa, which I find soaks it up. I bought preshredded cheddar, which wasn't as flavorful as a nicer cheese would have been. Next time, I'll go for something better with more bite and probably use less quinoa. Otherwise I wouldn't change a thing.

We loved this. I did roast the broccoli first then chopped. Not a fan of raw broccoli.

My husband wants to try it with buckwheat groats next time.

Got started on this, made dressing and quinoa.  Decided to cut recipe in half for two people, subbed sliced almonds (untoasted) and raisins, as what were available.  It was ok but maybe better the next day.  Can see where toasted pecans and dried cranberries would add something.
This salad got better with a few days in the fridge and the addition of our family garlic/tarragon vinegar salad dressing. Without the dressing for moisture and flavor, a bit bland.
Yummy. I’ll probably add bacon pieces to the leftovers, and then it will be perfect.
I made this and used bulgar for the grain. This made a huge amount of salad. It's great but it serves way more than 6.

A bright and satisfying meal!  And I do mean meal, I used farro instead of quinoa because that’s what we had in house (trying to “shop” my own pantry lately). Also substituted roasted cashews for pecans, but only by sprinkling on top with each serving, because we like the crunch.  I would maybe add the whole lemon’s juice next time. And I would definitely encourage using the raw broccoli chopped very finely, the texture is just a really nice crunch. Nice summery salad in the middle of winter!

Feeds four? This salad is so good I could eat the entire bowl myself.  Actually, there is no end of substitutions one could make. I happened to have a large container of cooked bulgur and quinoa on hand so I used  that. I think farro would be equally excellent.  The broccoli does not at all taste raw.
This salad is surprisingly delish!  Its a keeper!  Even before adding the apple and cheese it was absolutely tasty.

I rated this recipe 3 Star because I have never made quiona before.  I followed the not so great directions... in STEP 1.  Did what it said and had mushy quionoa.  So, I looked on quiona bag directions that is specific about amount of water and used them.  So much better.  Also the recipe ingredients should say 1 cup quinoa, COOKED and rinsed.
I've made this 'spoon salad' twice and like it very much. However, it produces an enormous salad, much more than 4 - 6 portions, more like 8 - 10. Just sayin'!
Very nice; balanced.  The cheddar and toasted pecans are especially interesting.  Can think of a lot of additions and tweaks, but it’s great as-is.

Mint
Parsley
More lemon
I love this salad. It has a great taste, is easy to make and holds well. The apples add a nice flavor.

So easy, delicious and filling — loved how it was all mixed in the same bowl. Agree with some of the other notes that more dressing wouldn’t hurt! This isn’t a critique of the recipe but I used average-quality cheddar and springing for fancier cheese would’ve made it even better. I could see adding golden raisins or grilled chicken, too.
Healthy, easy, but not very tasty.
This was great! My hack was pearl couscous. I chopped the broccoli into small pieces and let the dressing work on the raw broccoli for about 3 hours, then cubed extra sharp cheddar.

I’ve made this twice now, once with cashews and once with pecans. I eat broccoli because it’s good for me, but don’t really like it. This salad is a tasty fresh and brilliant way to get your broccoli! Chop it very fine, and as others have said, drop it into the dressing while you get on with the rest. Definitely include the cheese and the cranberries, as well as the apple. Use any nuts you have to hand. It needs the lemon. It keeps really well so great for lunch boxes over several days.
An excellent main course salad. It's a lot of laborious chopping, but it makes for prodigious amount of food, and even when dressed, the salad lasts quite well.

I loved this salad even though I had never cooked quinoa and didn't know I needed a fine mesh strainer LOL.  I made the biggest mess I have ever made without my granddaughter. I made it exactly as written only with a little less quinoa.
Interesting flavors but absolutely not worth the repetitive stress injury from finely chopping all that broccoli.
Delicious and so easy and fast! Very flavorful. Agree with others for us this is more of a side than main dish- we had with roasted salmon. But looking forward to having with lunch this week too!

This is a great, make ahead lunch recipe for the week…however I *highly* recommend doubling the dressing recipe—it’s just too dry otherwise. Oh, and I second what someone else said about using the Trader Joe’s Unexpected Cheddar!

This is a great fall/winter salad. I do enjoy raw broccoli, but this time I chopped it very finely so that it could macerate in the dressing. In fact, I chopped everything for this salad very finely. I was lacking a few ingredients and used Whole Foods Super Grains instead of quinoa, pineapple vinegar and dried lemon peel for the lemon, and both cheddar and swiss cheese. Oh, and dried cherries for the cranberries--I get "no sugar added" dried cherries from Michigan. Otherwise exactly as written!
The kiddo doesn’t like quinoa so made with farro - so tasty. Made a second time, still with farro, but riffed on the dressing using miso etc. such a tasty, easy and versatile dinner!!

What a terrific salad! I, too, I am not a fan of raw broccoli, but this salad is absolutely delicious. I did find that I needed to make a little extra dressing. I also prefer to grate the cheese on top of individual servings.

I used faro instead of quinoa, and loved the nutty chew that it provided. I only had 12oz of broccoli on hand so I stretched it by adding a can of rinsed chickpeas. Great recipe. Very filling and satisfying for a winter lunch.

This was great and super easy to put together. I did have to make a few substitute rooms because I didn’t have the exact ingredients, but found it to be stellar anyway! I didn’t have lemons so I used a cara cara orange and I didn’t have block cheddar so I used shredded cheese. Lastly, I didn’t have pecans, so I used toasted almond slices. It’s nice to know it’s very forgiving on the subs. My husband was impressed and can’t stop eating it. Love getting a nice dose of protein in my salad!
Amazingly delicious!  Made it precisely as directed using extra sharp cheddar (very small cubes) and it was awesome. Will add this to our repertoire.

I forgot to buy broccoli so I substituted shaved Brussels sprouts and it was delicious!
Great recipe!
all the textures here are perfect! the dressing perfectly tempers the raw broccoli. this would be really good with other grains, too!

Attractive & versatile dish. I made it per recipe except 1) used dried cherries instead of cranberries (all I had - cut the larger ones in half); 2) used mozzarella (all I had - not a fan of cheddar); 3) stirred in some nutritional yeast as a nod to the missing cheddar. Next time (and there will definitely be one) I'll omit cheese and cheese sub altogether. I'm always looking for easy, nutritious side dishes that also work as main entree for family vegetarians and this fits the bill.
I’ve made this twice now and think it’s a very tasty, healthy feeling salad. I steamed the broccoli just until bright green and only use half a cup of quinoa. Easy and tasty!
I made this with brown rice (but without Cheddar because I forgot!) and loved it. I'd never had broccoli raw before, it's delicious. I'll definitely do it more.
Delicious, but needs more dressing: I’ll try 1 1/2 times dressing next time.

Delicious salad and thought the raw broccoli worked perfectly once marinaded in the dressing. Was worried about insufficient dressing per comments so did heaping measurements. Don’t recommend for the Dijon as it became a dominant flavour with too much heat.  Otherwise, I thought the ratios in the recipe worked perfectly.
Very tasty! Subbed raisins for the dried cranberries (it’s what I had) so added more lemon juice to keep things tart. Also had some fresh mint on hand and mixed that in—was a nice addition.
This sounded so good but the “aggressively salted” quinoa made the whole dish way too salty.
I had it with Farro. Amazing,even though I hate raw broccoli in general!
very good, though could use an herb—parsley, arugula, or sliced green onion maybe. added flax seeds.

This was super yummy, make a LOT, and is very filling! The only thing I had to swap was Parmesan cheese for the cheddar and golden raisins for the cranberries, but otherwise I followed the recipe and it worked out great. The raw broccoli softened overnight and was the perfect crunchy texture by the time I ate it for lunch the next day. Will definitely be adding this into my rotation!
This was really good! I decided to roast the broccoli and used a mystery grain from my pantry (hulled millet?). Next time I will double the dressing because it was so yummy.
I have made this recipe with couscous and with quinoa. l liked it better with couscous. The texture was more chewy than mushy.
Made exact to recipe. Served the next day. I would not serve this immediately after making. It improves with a bit of time.

The idea of the salad and ingredients sounded great, but for me there was a bit much going on. My husband really liked it yet I found the mustard to be too forward, as well as the dressing too tart. I think if I were to make it again, I would eliminate or reduce the mustard and reduce the lemon. Loved the broccoli, nuts, cheese, and quinoa, and would try different varieties and combinations of nuts, cheese, grain, and dried fruit to mix it up.

I made this exactly as written and it’s delicious. The broccoli did soften a bit after sitting for a while and lost that totally raw texture. Would definitely make again and wouldn’t bother cooking the broccoli.

LOVED LOVED LOVED this! Only had a few dried cranberries left, so subbed in currants for the remainder of the dried fruit (thought they worked fine). Took others' advice and doubled the dressing (loved how tangy it was); used 1/2-2/3 of it on top of what was called for. Next time I might use more apple (with red peel for contrast; this time I used a Granny Smith). I think this would go well at a pot-luck, as it made a LOT. Good thing my 17yo son likes it! :-)
Add Feta cheese at serving
Love this recipe. It really doesn't feel/taste like raw broccoli. Bonus: it's prepared with one bowl, one measuring cup, one measuring spoon. The chopping took a while but clean up was easy!

I made this exactly per the instructions and really liked the flavors a lot (and agree with everyone who said that cooking the broccoli is not necessary). I am, however, still trying to figure out (not for lack of effort on my part) what quinoa brings to the table. I think I'll make this again but will substitute farro for the quinoa.

Thank you Sohla! I've been looking for long lasting salads with ingredients I'm likely to have around. This is going to be on my WFH rotation. I like the way Quinoa gets lodged into the broccoli florets. With each chomp you get a heat of the mustard, crunch and sweetness of the apple, chewyness of the cranberry and cheese. Thank you so much. Been running out of things to eat while I work from home.
Love this salad.  Has become a “gamer salad”. Everyone I’ve made this for that is cool with broccoli has asked me for the recipe!
Pretty good.
We used Farro and it was great.

I’ve made this several times & would like to have a bowl right now!  Good potluck dish, stays tasty for several days, healthy—you can’t go wrong.  The only changes I made is to skip the lemon zest but increase the juice.  I also halved the amount of quinoa.

I like this recipe but also nit a fan of raw broccoli. Zapped it in the microwave for 2 minutes with a little water, then cooled and drained. On hand substitutions: dried cherries and ricotta salata. Added chopped scallions.

To me, this is super tasty! Like others said, this is in no way a 6 serving situation. But I’m fine with that as I’m always on the lookout for vegetarian potluck recipes. I cooked the quinoa in 2 cups of water, seasoned with Better than Bouillon vegetable base as per brand instructions, until the quinoa was done and the water evaporated. Used juice from the whole lemon.

Delicious! Endlessly riff-able! I’ve had it as written and enjoyed (perhaps didn’t love the 3rd day texture of the cheddar). Made it recently with Parmesan instead and that held up better (chopped it into tiny nubs), subbed dried cherries for the cranberries and toasted walnuts for the pecans. Love it!
Took to a party and the bowl was empty after nearly everyone took seconds.
This was so good!  I used pumpkin seeds instead of pecans, because that's what I had on hand, and it was very good.
Left out the cheese and honey. Bumped up the amount of oil and vinegar to compensate. Also added 1/2 Tbs of grainy mustard.

I was intrigued by this recipe but feared it would be tough on the digestive system…have no fear, it’s delicious! Use the sharpest cheese you can find, and Granny Smith apples to balance. It makes a lot but keeps well.
Add dill

Made for a Memorial Day celebration and left broccoli raw and generally got good feedback. I would not parboil as others recommend. It’s good, but will add fresh herbs and/or thinly sliced shallot in the future
Made as written and it was delicious.  Next time I going to try feta in place of cheddar.
Yum!  I would 1 1/2 times the dressing next time.  I love raw broccoli and it does soften up when added to the dressing.
This recipe was amazing and easy. Yum.
The star here is the dressing. Made it to a tee. Fantastic dish.

Made this recipe exactly as stated, and it is awesome. Lasted multiple days in the fridge for lunches. I kept the Cheddar, cranberries, and pecans separate and just added them to the single serving dish each day so they wouldn't get soggy or slimy. This will definitely be added to the lunch rotation! (Don't cook the broccoli!)
I think this salad would’ve been perfect without the cheddar or maybe with a different kind of cheese

A surprising and excellent salad.  The honey mustard dressing pairing with the raw broccoli is inspired.  It makes a lot of salad.  Perfect for a hot day. I will make it again but will probably skip the cheese- it didn’t feel necessary- and I might skip the quinoa too
This salad is perfection. So easy to make, healthy and delicious. A great lunch meal. I even stopped adding the cheese because it doesn't really need it.

Made as is using a Pink Lady apple and Sharp White Cheddar from Tillamook. Topped with Morningstar Chickn strips and it was a good, filling dinner. Not a typical recipe for me, but I trust Sohla’s recipe testing!
This salad is so good.  I chose to not add the cheese.  I did not cook the broccoli.  I've made this four times already.
Outstanding. Used maple syrup instead of honey, doubled the dressing and the broccoli, slivered almonds. Perfect lunch meal prep for hot summer days.

My husband and I really liked this salad. I made 1/2 the recipe and had plenty for the two of us. I wouldn’t recommend parboiling the broccoli, the flavor of the raw broccoli is brighter and you’d lose some of the delightful crunch. My husband wants more cheese and more pecans, but I think it’s perfect as it is. The dressing would be great on any number of fruit or vegetable salads.
This is my go to salad, and I eat it for days. The dressing is to die for
Endlessly adaptable depending on what you have on hand. Makes a perfect meal prep recipe because it keeps so well. Was skeptic about the raw broccoli but it's great. Definitely a keeper!
Has anyone tried using a different grain? Maybe farro?
An all-time favorite. Perfect for days when you do not want to heat up the kitchen for anything.

I’ve watched Sohla El-Waylly since her time at the test kitchen and time and time again she makes amazing recipes with ingredient combinations I would’ve never tried before. Only change I made was that I boiled the broccoli and this meal was great!

Fresh and easy - the perfect ball to school picnic salad!  People were loving this, and many wanted the recipe.  I doubled it with no problem, and one family took it home in a ziploc bag!  I didn’t have the cranberries, so I used dried cherries (cut in half), and it was a “no nuts” picnic, so I omitted the pecans. So good, I can’t wait to make it again.
Made this for a few friends and am going to make it for a very large dinner party this weekend, it's fantastic!

Thanks Sohla for setting the foundation for these flavors. My cooking creativity led me to use the instant pot and oven. 1 cup rinsed quinoa goes into the instant pot with 1 1/4 cups water. Cook on high for 1 minute. Let sit for 15 as steam naturally releases. I enjoy adding turmeric and black pepper for a gentle flavor and nutrition boost while the grains cook. Additionally I roasted the broccoli and added some sweet potato. Cheers to a satisfying bowl of goodness!

Super good and easy to make. Do NOT par-cook the broccoli. You can do little add-ins here on things that you like - I added some chopped dates, which gives it some nice sweetness. Once it sits overnight you will need to add more oil vinegar because those seem to soak in and be less prevalent the next day, but holds up great over a few days in the fridge.
What could be substituted for the Dijon mustard? Grated ginger root or Horseradish?
Delicious salad, I wouldn’t put as much quinoa in the next time.
I made this last night and it was delicious. I chopped the broccoli into very small pieces. I loved the flavor of the apple with the honey mustard dressing.
As much as I try, just don't like raw broccoli, however after a quick blanch in boiling water and then an Ice water plunge and drain, It works much better in my very humble opinion.

Used barley and raisins instead of quinoa and cranberries, they were what I had and worked well.  Reluctantly left out the cheese as I'm in need of iron, some excellent sources in this but dairy inhibits iron absorption.
Changed a few things for my taste but loved this. Dressing amazing with broccoli/apple/cranberry combo

Just made it! Sent a pic to my foodie friends and a recipe link. Looking forward to it tasting even better after it sits awhile. Used Farro as had it on hand. Otherwise I followed the recipe and forsee this being on a regular rotation. None of the ingredients go out of season for the most part. Agree that the cheddar really sells this as well as toasted pecans, and, the dressing! It's delicious!

Like that the dressing is so unique.  Made in Thursday and it held up well over the weekend for good snacking.  All that broccoli chopping was a bit tiresome, but worth it.  Made a faro since we don’t like quinoa

I made the dressing per the recipe, but then subbed the mix-ins for what I had on hand (excluded apple and pecans, added carrots, cashews, and raisins). It turned out great! I think the core quinoa, broccoli, and dressing are all you need and you can experiment from there. A versatile salad :)
Would this work with raisins instead of dried cranberries?
