Unsuccessful for me, too-- trying to figure out why, giving Sohla a little benefit of the doubt. Wouldn't the KIND of PB have a huge bearing on texture and taste? The fat level of PB varies widely, doesn't it? And if you are using shelf-stable PB, would stabilizers and emulsifiers influence results? I am surprised the recipe does not advise about what KIND of peanut butter to use.

I made this spontaneously cause I didn’t know what to eat for breakfast. Indeed they tasted a little bland. But with some jam or yoghurt added they were fine. I didn’t use so much sweetener and less salt. And they were a little dry in the end but nice sort of banana bread vibe.

Love Sohla El-Waylly's recipes but something terrible happened here. I note the recipe is "updated" Jan 17, 2024. Sad to say the entire product had to be trashed. The only good thing is the beautiful picture.

My husband and I really enjoyed these! Not sure what the less satisfied bakers did. I used a little less olive oil than what the recipe calls for - about 4 tbsp instead of 6 - but otherwise followed it to the letter. I wonder if maybe folks were expecting something with the sweetness and texture like a cake, but this recipe is intended to be for breakfast bars! So yes, they are fudgier and not quite as sweet as a cake or bread. They are tasty and satisfying!

I was very happy with the taste and texture. I loved the peanut butter/banana flavor combo. I reduced the olive oil to 5T (and will try to get away with 4T next time). I also cut back the honey to 4T as my peanut butter already contained sugar. I used raw honey. I used 1/2 tsp of fine salt, not a full teaspoon, and only one tsp of cinnamon because I find cinnamon can overpower everything.  Definitely will make these again and enjoy more experimenting.

Well I thought these were lovely! Made sure I used overripe bananas. The only sub I made was Bobs Redmill vegan egg replacer to sub the eggs, and still found the flavor to be the perfect light sweetness for breakfast. I used 1.5 tsp of an off brand salt (are people complaining about saltiness following the Diamond Crystal recc?) and didn’t add the top salt. Maybe this recipe just isn’t for those who prefer a traditionally sweet breakfast, but it’s a great grab and go option for me!

I was almost put off by the mixed reviews, but that also challenged me to make this exactly as written to find out for myself... and as always, those bananas to use up.  The negative reviews,  prepared me for the results: Not dessert, not banana bread... but satisfying, healthy breakfast bites.    My four year old grandson who has started to be food choosy, happily had seconds.  I don't seek out gluten-free or vegan, but this is a bonus for those who do.
Followed recipe; three tasters agreed "just not good" despite all the healthy ingredients.
Mediocre at best. Gloopy and no taste of banana. Would benefit from more fruit and some flour for cohesiveness. Had to increase bake time to 50 min and still underdone. Skip it.
These were just terrible.  They went in the trash after a bite from my wife and I.  Cooked to a T, and we don't take throwing food in the trash lightly. How did this make the #1 recipe today?!

Not good. Followed the recipe and added a handful of chocolate chips, but it came out both bland and salty. Double checked to confirm that I’d added the correct amount of the right kind of salt. But I think this’ll go right into the compost.
Found these bvery salty and little else. A disappointment.

How could this list of wonderful ingredients make such a Meh bars?  What’s wrong , I don’t know everything.  I spent time thinking of how to improve: dried fruit, nuts in the batter, chocolate chips.  Finally the conclusion i came to was find a different recipe.
These were way too salty and terrible. Waste of time, effort and cleaning dishes!

I agree with everyone who writes that these are too salty, and flavourless otherwise. The texture is off, too. One of the most disappointing NYT recipes I have made so far. They are so wrong that I, too, looked over the ingredient list once they had cooled and I had tasted one. I wanted to rinse my mouth out afterwards. They leave a cloying oily and salty taste that lingers. Ugh.

I don't usually write reviews, but I thought this time I should.  What a disappoint this recipe is. It certainly is easy and it smelled great coming out of the oven, but it was tasteless, except for salt.  And that was even with adding chocolate bits!   I ended up throwing it out.
Don't waste your time. I followed this recipe exactly. This combination doesn't taste good. Too salty. Texture is not good. I'm not sure what would help it.
Husband made these tonight and they were basically inedible. Seriously too much salt, and the texture was just weird. A sad waste of a lot of ingredients . . .

These were way too salty. I halved the salt based on comments and they were still too salty. Otherwise I made them exactly as specified and found them to be too oily and not particularly tasty. One of the worst recipes I've made from NYT Cooking.
I don’t know what happened! It tasted like salty nothingness - all of the ingredients somehow canceled out. Love Sohla’s other recipes but this was a miss for me.
Came out salty but otherwise bland. An unfortunate waste of ingredients.
So salty, and the texture was like a half baked cake. Followed the recipe exactly and I did bake for the full 40 min.  Ended up in compost as we couldn’t eat it.

I made these twice, bc the recipe is exactly the kind of thing I am always looking for and I had forgotten on round 2 that I had already tried this once. Really disappointed, both times. Do not taste good despite the ingredients being ones I'm excited about. Texture is claggy despite ensuring a thorough cook. And do not keep well, get soggy within a day.

I rarely leave comments, but this was sadly just not good. I made it exactly as the recipe, and found it incredibly oily and tasteless. I wanted to like this, but it wasn’t worth the ingredients or time - save your bananas for banana bread!

Overall, really like these for a grab no go brekkie. Especially as someone newly gluten free. Used half honey, half maple syrup since honey was running low. 
Has anyone done this with coconut instead of the walnuts on top? I feel like the walnuts are a little too heavy for the fluffy texture of the rest of the bar. Next time I’ll tray large shredded coconut instead.
We aren't too impressed with this recipe. The peanut butter overwhelmed the banana but did not have the yumminess of a peanut butter cookie. I won't make it again.

I should have read the reviews - these are so bad. They’re salty and tasteless. My husband asked me if I accidentally melted plastic into them. I did not. I refuse to waste food, so I will eat them - resentfully.
This is not a good recipe. These are greasy, salty and not delicious. One of the worst recipes I've seen on NYT cooking.
These were just plain bad.

Added extra over ripe bananas (which was the raison d'etre for the recipe), used almond butter, flaky salt on top, served warm, definitely captured the attention of all my co-workers and they really enjoyed that it wasn't that sweet. The squares held together well. I think it was a good choice over the usual banana bread which was going to take too long to do in the morning before work and didn't require to be slathered with butter or cream cheese:) ! Thank you for the idea.

I followed the recipe and thought these were really good. I’m not sure what all the fuss is about with them being too salty. Mine turned out perfect. Perhaps people are using salted peanut butter?? These tasted like healthy banana and peanut butter bread in bar version, which matched my expectations. Also came together super easily and fast, so bonus points for that.

Followed the recipe exactly, it came out a weirdly flavorless oily meh, and a waste of ingredients. Almost composted it but decided to put it outside for the raccoons instead. They ate them, but that's hardly a high bar.

what do you all eat for breakfast every day? pancakes and bacon? this is a perfectly tasty breakfast for folks who like to start their day with something healthy.

subbed coconut oil for olive oil. 1/2 eggs was a flax egg because I only had 1 chicken egg.
I found these bars delicious and can’t believe others called them bland. They have a wonderful honey nut flavour. I highly recommend them.

The first time I made these they were not nearly as flavorful as they smelled while I was baking them. The second time I made them I decided to apply a generous amount of salt at the end to sprinkle on top before putting it into the oven and found it made all the difference! I do not agree with other reviews that it was too salty but in fact the opposite. More salt adds a nice contrast between the sweet of the banana/honey.

Don’t know what everyone’s complaining about. I think they’re tasty. Not too salty. Pretty strong on the peanutty flavour. A nice healthyish morning bite. I did run out of honey and sub in some maple syrup.
Yum!!  Made these w pure almond butter (pure=ground almonds. No added yucky oils) plus an extra tsp of cinnamon. Baked at convection 340 for 27 minutes.

Reading these reviews and trying to decide if I dare make this recipe. I have a hunch that people’s choice of nut butter might be the swing variable. The question would then become *which* nut butter is the best fit for a happy outcome?
I made these exactly as written, and they came out perfectly! They make the perfect pre-workout snack! So yummy!!

These were really good - not sure what the other home cooks did. I used a maple walnut pecan cashew butter and added an extra 25g oats. I skipped the salt. 

Poured them onto a sheetpan and baked. You could probably bake this in a loaf pan. Mine came out like banana bread.
I was nervous after all the comments but went ahead with it. I omitted all added salt, used super ripe bananas, and underbaked a tad. Perfect!! Moist, delicious, and filling. Will make again!

I made these vegan with an egg substitute, reduced the oil to just 2 tbl, subbed maple syrup for the honey and added 4 finely chopped dates. Upped the cinnamon and added some five spice too. Family loved.

I reduced the kosher salt slightly because the brand we have in the house is saltier; not all kosher salt is the same. Also used a natural peanut butter that has only peanuts and salt. Taste is good to me. Lots of negative comments, but I am glad to have a substitute for packaged granola or protein bars that I sometimes grab on the way to work.

I don’t understand the negative reviews here. If you want a muffin, then just make the darn muffin. If you want a scone, then just go make or buy a scone.  To each their own I guess. I am on a high fiber diet and this recipe checks off a lot of boxes for me and it’s also not loaded with sugar. I ended up going with almond butter and cut out almost all the oil by subbing apple sauce. I did end up adding several diced and pitted Medjool dates and that added a wonderful texture and density akin to a packaged breakfast bar. I stored in an airtight container during the week and noticed that the flavor developed as these sat longer. I would go with riper bananas next time and am thinking of subbing out the eggs with hydrated chia seeds and maybe adding some Greek yogurt for added flavor.

Delicious. A bit of common sense will have you read the recipe first and halve the amount of salt. I've made this twice and find it flavourful, fudgy and highly adaptable - once I didn't have oats and used plain flour, another time i didn't have honey and used soft brown sugar, and bot times it turned out fantastic.

I followed the instructions exactly, except I baked it 10 more minutes because center looked wet. The texture is fudgy and it tastes like an unsweetened blondie with a salty top. Next time, I’ll add mini chocolate chips or coconut flakes on top.
Not bad, but a little too salty. Next time I’ll probably add only half the salt and skip the salt sprinkle on top. I also added a few chocolate chips on top to intice my kids to try something new.
What amount of chocolate chips did you add Miranda?  That sounds like a great addition to me!
I just want to say thank you to the community who makes time to provide info and improve recipes! It is appreciated!
These were pretty bad. Too salty, not sweet at all, no banana flavor. Really disappointed.

Awful.  I made it with almond butter.  I used 4 tbsps of olive (not 6 tbsps), used half of the salt noted, used a bit of nutmeg as I didn’t have any cinnamon.  The taste (granted with nutmeg) was bland and still too salty.  I would not make this again.

Not good. It comes out bland and salty, even when I added a handful of chocolate chips. I even double checked to ensure I’d out of the correct amount of the right type of salt. This will go straight to the compost, sadly.

This recipe was awful. I used flax eggs bc of an egg allergy, but even with eggs, it’s hard to imagine this ever tasting good. It was incredibly salty! Omit the salt on top and maybe this recipe will improve, but it’s not sweet and like others are commenting, it’s almost savory and bland. I don’t want to try this recipe again, but if I did, I’d add heaping amounts of cinnamon and extra sweetness more honey or brown sugar perhaps. Better off just baking banana bread…
Agree with American in Paris, I followed recipe and just not very tasty. If you’re going to try them, skip the salt on top for sure, I think took away from the flavor of the honey and peanut butter.
very disappointing. wasted very good ingredients. Flavorless except for the overwhelming saltiness. Save your ingredients and make something else!

I suspect there are at least two key reasons some report dissatisfaction with their output: one, they didn’t time a full 15 minutes for the banana-egg mix and missed out on that chemical reaction; two, they didn’t try the bars the next day. I bet the flavor improves over a day or two. Or their oil was old. Or their walnuts were stale or not toasted lightly first. Anyhow, my panful smells better and fuller than any banana bread or cake. Can’t wait to cut into them tomorrow morning.

Okay! The morning after, finally tasted these bars. What a unique, delicious variation on banana bakes. Agreed with the baker who called them akin to Lara bars: that cheek-by-jowl, peanut butter-banana combo is spot on. Texture is fluffy and will be familiar to those who make those two-ingredient banana pancakes. I look forward to their slight maturing in flavor in a day or so. Used sorghum instead of honey, BTW - out of honey. Really good!
Too salty if using PB or nut butter with salt already added. Adjust accordingly! Love the texture of these. Will make again.
Way too salty
Agreed with other commenters who found these bland. They are also oily in texture. I wouldn’t wake up craving one of these!
Similar to what other commenters wrote, I found these incredibly bland. Only the salt really came through. They’re also calorie dense - so I did not find them worth the effort or the calories.

After reading all the comments, I made this, substituting with what I had on hand: Skippy peanut butter and a scant 1/4 c. olive oil; no salt; 1 T. pumpkin pie spice, used 4 T. maple syrup, 1 T. dark agave syrup and 1 T. brown sugar for sweetener; sprinkled the top with chopped honey-roasted peanuts. I waited until it was completely cooled to taste it. I think the texture is really luscious and the flavor is subtle but really good. A keeper for me!

@Cate I make this every couple of months. Last time, I used almond butter, white sugar, almond flavoring, and sprinkled the top with sliced almonds. Its a very forgiving, flexible recipe that we enjoy.

Few points:
- Indeed, the texture is nice and fudgy, but if I had a hankering for something banana-nut flavored, I wouldn’t turn to this. 
- Used handmade chunky peanut butter and can barely detect it, unfortunately. 
- While the squares aren’t bad I wouldn’t make this for guests or for part of a special brunch. 
- Really glad I skipped the additional salt. Even though I’m a fan of salty things, the 1 tsp of table salt was almost too much. I’d do 3/4 or a 1/2 plus the flaky salt on top next time
Based on comments I was unsure how these would turn out- but they turned out great! Added pecans instead and folded them into the batter along chocolate chips. Will make again!
I actually liked these a lot more than it seems others did. Incredibly simple to whip up. Took the tip to add chocolate chips. Delicious.

I thought these were really good. Based on the comments about lack of flavor, I added semi-sweet chocolate chips but I think the flavor would have been great without them. I might do half and half in the future. I didn't add the salt on top and liked them without it.

Loved the uniqueness of this recipe but WOAH are they salty. Perhaps my brand of peanut butter was saltier than Sohla’s. I’ve been looking for a nice and dense, portable, high calorie but homemade snack for hiking and this really fits the bill! I loved they texture best of all- lots of homemade granola bars are impossibly crumbly. Next time I’ll at least halve the salt, and I want to try with blended dates instead of honey.

I rather liked these. For anyone who didn't know this already, use about half as much Morton's kosher salt than Diamond Crystal. I used sea salt this time (vs table salt) and was happy with the result. I also used a natural peanut butter. I half wondered if I could use Nutella, but didn't try it. I will probably try a little less oil next time to compensate for what's in the natural peanut butter. These have more fat than I'd like for breakfast but they are yummy!
Was excited to make these for my toddler son and I to have as a morning snack but they are almost inedible by how salty they are. It’s a shame and a big waste of time and ingredients.
I kept checking the ingredient list to see if I missed something...this turned out terrible, I had to throw it away.  Totally wet and mushy even after baking an extra 10 minutes.

Based on others' comments about flavor, I added chocolate chips and thought these were really good. I think they would have been good without the chocolate chips. It's subtle flavor. In the future I would do half with chocolate chips and half without.

These were great with some tweaks. I used three bananas instead of two because the first two were well short of a cup when mashed. I also added chopped dates. These baked for a little longer than the recipe said, about 45 minutes in all. A solid make-ahead, pack-ahead breakfast.
Baked in a muffin pan for 25 minutes and they came out perfect

I loved these bars! 
I doubled this recipe as I wanted to make use of 4 unripe bananas. Substituted maple syrup for honey and used only about 8 tbsp. 
Also did not completely double the unsalted almond butter requirement. 

However, I listened to the comments and reduced the amount of salt. I truly wish I had trusted Sohla as she knows how to please the Asian palate. I added flaky salt on top but it was just short of being enough.

I also added fresh ground cardamom and nutmeg to my recipe.

Why didn't I read the comments first?? Made a double recipe to get rid of some over ripe bananas and these are WAY TOO SALTY. AM just throwing them out. Dang - that's a lot of wasted honey, almond butter and olive oil $$$

These were not very flavorful at all, as others have noted. I would recommend reducing the salt by at least half and not throwing any on top. Also, don’t use natural peanut or almond butter because it makes them too soft.

These have potential but as is they are pretty terrible. We threw them away.
This was so salty I threw it out. I’m going to stick with banana bread.

This is an awful recipe. It has no banana taste, even though I used banana extract, but mostly, it  had way too much oil and the finished product was greasy. The bars also were quite soft, and not crisp, as I had expected.  Don’t waste your time and ingredients on this (although I do usually like NYT recipes).
I scored the recipe low because as others have noted, there really wasn’t any taste despite the ingredients.
Agree with other reviewers that there’s too much salt and no overarching flavor. I hate that I used a $10 jar of almond butter on this. Won’t make again.

The salt was to taste,  so I went light on the salt. The only thing i did differently on the recipe was use 2.5 bananas to get a full cup. I won't make these again. The olive oil flavor permeates. I used cold pressed virgin. Very light tasting. The end product was totally disgusting.
Lining the pan with parchment paper and overlapping the sides made it very easy to lift these out of the pan and into the garbage. Mostly tasteless with hints of straw.

This was incredibly salty and mushy. Please heed the warnings by other reviewers to decrease the salt by half or more. We used kosher salt too, so we didn’t think it would be that overpowering (unfortunately, it was). Trust your instincts—if it seemed like the salt was too much when making this, you’re likely right. If you enjoy moist baby food, this is your ticket (although with the high sodium content, I wouldn’t feed this to any kid or adult with HBP).

I found this recipe absolutely delicious as is - perfect for breakfast. I suspect those who found it bland or salty were expecting the sweetness of muffins or a typical sweetbread, which this is not. They're super moist and satisfying without being too sweet. If that's what you're looking for in breakfast, these are for you.

I’m not understanding the other comments about no flavour and too much salt. I didn’t have kosher salt so I followed the recipe of 1tsp fine salt and thought it was perfect. I also loved the topping of flaky salt. I love the fudge texture and the walnuts on top to contrast. I could taste the sweetness, banana, peanut butter, and a hint of olive oil. I love the balance of flavours in the recipe. A great treat to start the day.

I reviewed the other notes and made a few changes.  I used brown sugar instead of honey and added some mini chocolate chips and a little coconut.  We really enjoyed it.  Our bananas were very ripe which added to the sweetness.  Will make it again for sure
These taste of olive oil and salt, not banana, honey or peanut butter.  Bland and not worth repeating.  Rather disappointing.

Halfway through mixing the batter I scrolled down and read the notes, and thought “yikes, oh well, might as well go for it”. So I had low expectations, but I was happily surprised. I think these are great! Just the right amount of sweetness. I used quick oats because that’s all I had. I love the walnuts on top, will use more next time. Used just a pinch of salt.

I think the outcome of this depends greatly on whether your peanut butter is good or not. Because all I can taste is subpar peanut butter, but I guess that is kind of on me. Chocolate/chips would make it better.
This recipe was very disappointing.   Nobody in my family liked it.  I think it really needs flour.

These bars are healthful and delicious and my 2 year old loves them. Yes, they’re not that sweet and the salt comes through. And yes, 2 bananas makes them less bananaey than you might have wanted… if you just don’t like the taste go make something else, don’t call it a bad recipe lol. Sohla rules. Coarse ground almond butter here and a flavorful olive oil, just 100g honey, and pecans - legit
Cooking time for me was much longer than recipe called for.
agree with all the comments on salt- wished that I would have only use 1/2 teaspoon-I unfortunately followed the recipe and the salt ruined the taste and the dish

This came out great! I went by the weights in the recipe. It did take a bit more than 2 bananas to get to 240g for me, which of course depends on the size of the bananas. I used natural peanut butter that only contains peanuts, and I used unsalted walnuts. I imagine it would affect how salty and sweet this is otherwise.
Agree with the reviews.  Bland, bland, bland.  Love all of the ingredients so I anticipated that these would be delicious.  Very disappointed.  Make a banana walnut bread instead.

These smell amazing going but taste disappointing when they’re done. I was so excited to make this recipe and it was a let down. I thought about adding chocolate chips, and I wish I had. These just don’t have any distinct flavor. I’ll still eat them because I made them and don’t like wasting that much food. Hoping some warm butter will help.

The mess and hassle with the ingredients is one thing but these have no taste.  Based on the other reviews, a recipe like this should have never been included in the Sunday supplement this week of breakfast recipes.  I wish I would have read the reviews before I spent the time making.  In addition to no taste, too much salt.
I, too, am a very experienced baker. I usually get rave reviews from NYT recipes. I should have skipped this one. Too salty. Little taste. Nothing positive to recommend. Will not make again.

Ghastly, nobody ate more than one bite. Banana, peanut butter, vanilla, and cinnamon were completely   masked. I’ll retry with half the amount of oil, using a neutral oil instead of the olive oil, only 1/4 to 1/2  teaspoon of salt, and skipping the flaky salt on top.

Subbed coconut oil for the oil and maple syrup for honey. We were not especially impressed immediately after cooking but they were so much better flavor after a day! Will plan for this another time. Might add either golden raisins and/or coconut flakes next time. Great portable breakfast!

I should have done what I often tell others to do, and read the comments first! These are waaay too salty! Maybe because my peanut butter was unsweetened? But even then...excessive.  It will be a great excuse to slather maple butter on top before consuming, as otherwise, the nuttiness and texture were great.

made today and would not make again. i don’t hate them as much as most, but they’re pretty underwhelming. for me, they are not too salty, but they do veer salty. there just is not alot of flavor, despite two flavor-packed ingredients: banana and peanut butter. if you’re needing to use up a banana, here’s a better choice: https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1012904-banana-everything-cookies. That recipe works well with olive oil, sugar reduced to 1/2 cup and adding a handful of chia seeds.
I agree that they are too salty and bland - however the texture was perfect. Not sure what I would do to “fix” it

Meh at best, as per everyone in the family. Made them according to recipe. No real taste, too much cinnamon and vanilla, definitely too much salt, even though I cut that one in half. Came out mushy and crumbly at the same time, baked per instructions until a toothpick came out clean. The formula could probably use a small addition of a starchy binder and a dab of leavening, just to lift the very compact appearance and taste. Maybe someone will try it, to me it was a waste of good ingredients.

We liked them, but prefer something not too sweet and filling for breakfast or a mid day snack.  Used about 4T honey,coconut oil instead of olive oil,  a little more cinnamon, a little nutmeg, and half the salt per other reviews.  I did end up having to add about twice the oats to thicken up the batch, and I let it sit for an hour, so the oats oils thicken up, before popping it into the oven.

I love to bake & try new recipes.  Made these this morning and no one in our house enjoyed them.  I realized they were gluten-free and brought them over to a neighbor who is gluten-free and thought maybe she'd enjoy them?  Saw another neighbor along the way and offered one to her.  Neither she, nor my other neighbor (who both tried to be kindly polite) could stomach them.  On a positive note, my dog and each of the neighbors' dogs thought they were scrumptious.
no!  tastes awful.  don’t bother
Mushy and bland. Threw it away, seemed undercooked even though I baked it 20 minutes longer.

almost didn’t make this because of all the bad reviews, but I’m so glad I did because it was exactly what I was looking for: a wholesome breakfast treat that you can eat on the go! Maybe everyone was disappointed because they actually just wanted dessert?? I made a couple small tweaks and thought the result was so good AND they kept really nicely. The changes I made were:

6 tbsp melted butter instead of oil
Maple syrup instead of honey
1.5 cups oats 
Dried cherries

After reading these reviews, I suspect the challenge with this recipe is which peanut butter to use. There is a huge amount of variation in texture, taste, and ingredients, particularly salt and sugar. I won't cook a recipe calling for peanut butter unless it at least specifies whether to use natural or commercial varieties.
Way too much salt. I couldn’t eat them and wouldn’t serve them. I thought I must have made a mistake until I read the notes and saw all the similar comments.
Replaced the honey with 1/4 cup brown sugar and the olive oil with 4 tbsp coconut oil. Was not as bland as others described, or overly sweet
I love Sohla's recipes! This was great. I did reduce salt a tad and threw in some semi sweet chocolate chips, flax seeds and raisins. Yum
Oats are not suitable for Celiacs or anyone truly requiring a GF diet. This recipe should note this or be untagged "gluten free".

@Allison oats don’t contain gluten though. They can be contaminated with gluten during processing (farm, packaging factory). Some coeliacs are affected by avonin (sp) in oats but it isn’t specifically gluten, it’sa derivative I think

This is really bland. It doesn't taste of bananas, or nuts, it just tastes like something "healthy" that you'd buy in a health store a few decades ago. I followed the recipe closely, and I'm really disappointed. I threw half of my batch away.
I followed this recipe exactly. These bars were bland, oily and salty, and I thought the texture was more undercooked than fudgy.  I ate half of one & tossed the rest.

Wish I had read the reviews.  I think it is the almond/peanut butter that ruins these.  Also, using salted or unsalted almond or peanut butter makes a huge difference in the saltiness.  Mostly, though, they are not "fudgy" as promised and they don't taste very good.
The one time I failed to read the notes before making the recipe...regret. Agree with the comments about too much salt and little flavor. I give it a solid "meh." Removing from my "Recipe Box" now.
This recipe is way too salty. I used 1.5 tsps (rather than the two tsps called for) and they were still almost inedible.
Too salty. Kids did not like them.  Maybe ok with chocolate chips added, but definitely not a hit here with my fam.
Delicious but came out very goopy - would have added another 1/3 of oatmeal. I had to stick them in my freezer for them to set.
Way too much salt. Will try again with less salt and more bananas.

I loved these! After reading the comments, I omitted the salt. I think I could have used 1/2 tsp. I reduced the oil by 1 tbsp because my dough was looking gloppy. I mixed the walnuts into the dough, and also mixed in semi-sweet chocolate chunks. I had VERY ripe bananas. I think the chocolate is a must if you want these for a healthier sweet treat. The texture is similar to a dense carrot cake or pumpkin bar. My toddler likes them but they’re not rich enough for my older junk-food loving kids.
I think they’re delicious, have been making them every week with no edits to the recipe. A great replacement for the boxed breakfast bars I used to buy.
Added cocoa powder, chocolate chips, and chia seeds and it was delicious. Did not think it was too salty or bland with these additions
nice texture but too many flavours. Could not taste banana or peanut butter. I would remove cinnamon next time.
First batch as written, pretty lousy. But such a great nutritional profile! Second batch much better: Omit cinnamon, use 1/2 tsp. salt, chill before cutting.
P.S. 8x8 pan, stir walnuts in, mix batter before preheating oven. (So the oats have time to absorb some liquid.) With these revisions, it’s a keeper!

I thought these were delicious - the fudgy texture is great, and ripe (key word) bananas make this sweet along with the honey. Healthy but not low calorie. I think it accomplishes its mission to be a healthy, nutritious breakfast bread. The negative commenters must not be very used to this sort of baking. It's not meant to be very sweet.

overall i liked this... but...

a) always let your bananas get extra ripe, not just semi-ripe. they'll be sweeter and more 'bananay'

b) despite the comment above, i don't like overly sweet baked goods so i cut the amount of honey by ⅓

c) i also cut the amount of nut butter in half so the flavor doesn't overpower the bananas, which nut butters tend to do. i used PB w-no added sugar

d) to enhance the banana flavor even further, i added a tsp of banana extract, but that's just me.
I found these were not that flavorful until I added more salt on the top. Made all the difference! When you sprinkle salt on at the end you really have to be generous. @American in Paris
I despise bananas. What fruit could I substitute please?
I can't eat bananas. I'm thinking...mabye avocado?
Maybe sweet potato?
@Claude apple sauce?

Followed recipe almost exactly except for: 1) reduced the salt to 3/4 tsp, 2) added a 1/4 c of high quality chocolate chips and 1/2 c of unsweetened coconut, 3) used pecans instead of walnuts on top. I think they’re good! Let’s see what our teens say.

I made these because I grew up eating healthy foods, so bananas, honey, oats, etc seemed ideal. For many recipes  I never measure precisely, and tend to approximate, in this case what was left in the jar of my favorite raw, crunchy, sprouted almond butter. As for salt, just a few twists of the grinder (I actually did not even look at the amount of salt stated in the recipe). I loved the recipe..had a couple bites soon after it came out of the oven and thought it was really quite good!!

These weren't as awful as the comments may be, but mainly because I made some changes since I had doubts going into the recipe. I used three, not two, ripe bananas. I only used 5 TBS of olive oil and next time I'll cut that to 3 or 3 1/2 TBS.  Also, I used a pinch of Maldon salt both in the batter and atop the nuts; cutting way back on the salt. Only 4 TBS honey. I added a cloves with the cinnamon and about 1 1/4 cups oats, so it held together better. Next time, I think I'll add chopped dates.
I added chocolate chips and coconut and a third banana  - it was delicious!

I forgot to add the oil to these. I had used a little more banana so they’re still very moist. I added chocolate chips and flaked coconut. They’re pretty good! A little sticky but tasty. I was worried after reading all the negative comments and happily surprised.
Believe the reviews that these have no taste. And they are gooey, not a breakfast bar.  Threw them out.
Maybe it would be tastier to sub 1c. of white whole wheat flour in place of the oatmeal & use only one egg.  IMO, Oatmeal is flavorless no matter what you add to it.
Tastes very salty. Would cut that down.
I always read the comments first- what a mistake not doing that with this recipe! Throwing it away. Too salty, too bland.
@angie. Glad all I did was print it and read comments while doing so. It’s scrap paper now!
Replaced eggs with 2 tablespoons of flaxseed meal (grind 2 tablespoons of flaxseed and soak in 5 tablespoons of water for 15 mins), excellent egg substitute
Did not like this...only NYT recipe that I haven't liked so far.  Too much salt..not great flavor.
I added one finely grated carrot, which gave it a little more moisture and created a pleasant texture. My toddler is insane for these.

I accidentally forgot all of the oil in my haste. I  substituted pumpkin butter for the almond butter. I used overly ripe bananas  (I.e. blackened). I also used 5 TBSP of honey, and added the walnut right into the batter instead of on top. As well, I sprinkled chia seeds and cranberries  on top as well as a handful of semi-sweet chocolate chips. Overall, it is a little salty but not overpowering. The accidental omission of oil perhaps enhanced the other flavours?
There is noticeable banana flavour. As well, the incorporated chopped walnuts gives it some crunch. In summary , these bars now taste like a low-fat oatmeal “sweet and salty”  type of soft  granola bar.
I made these this morning and I thought I did something wrong because they were terrible.
I found the amount of oil to be very off-putting in the flavor. Too heavy, too sweet, not tasty at all. Bummed to have wasted all my ingredients on this. My kids didn’t like it either.

Just made this recipe and wished that I had read the reviews first. It looked great coming out of the oven, but tasted terrible. The texture was bad, too. The worst NYTimes recipe I've ever made. Unfortunately, I had to throw it out.
Way too salty, probably would be good if the salt was reduced by 1/2 or more. Not sure if these are going to be eaten. Maybe a little bit on top of ice cream? Sad to waste all those ingredients.

I checked the comments about 30 seconds after adding the salt - and agree this is WAAAYY too salty. (& I like salt!) TBH I can’t give much more of a review as I kept adding in SO. MANY. things trying to cover that up. More banana. Yogurt. Applesauce. Almond flout. Craisins. Another egg to balance all that. I took a tiny piece to try and couldn’t finish it. So it’s going in the bin. 
Sorry NYT this was not a win! 
(I do think the salt issue may be variable based on the type of peanut butter you use - most have salt added so if you were using unsalted it might be ok).

Do not waste your time or the food ingredient costs. I followed this to the letter and they were literally inedible. The only pro: the cooked product looked delicious out of the oven. That's it ... that's the pro. the cons: incredibly salty (and I used no-salt peanut butter,) the honey scorched (as so often happens in baked recipes... but I went against instinct and followed the recipe,) the texture was atrocious (gummy, pasty, stuck to roof of my mouth but not in a good "peanut butter" way,) the bars somehow both squished and crumbled (gummy squish with the cut edges falling into little pieces) when sliced. I usually keep opinions to myself regardless of whether I personally enjoyed the recipe because, hey, we're all doing the best we can and tastes are unique and personal. But these were SO BAD, I am shocked they made it into the app.
Worse, they made it into a calendar with daily recipes!
Yah I made this without reading the comments first. The texture is gross, and it is too salty. I would have vastly preferred making banana bread with my ripe naners rather than this dud.

Like Miranda, I agree that chocolate chips are a great addition!  I used light olive oil and because I used salted peanut butter, I cut the salt to 1 teaspoon and omitted the flaky salt. I also added 3/4 cup chocolate chips, used a full tablespoon of vanilla extract, and added in an additional 2 T of honey or sugar.  Without these tweaks, I found it to be a little meh -- not sweet, not savory.  But with these changes, it turns into a lovely gluten free, dairy free dessert!
I  followed the recipe exactly and had to throw the entire thing out. It was awful.
Delicious and simple. Used 1 tsp fine sea salt and 3 smallish very ripe bananas.

The negative reviews lead me to believe that people were hoping to bake a treat, like banana bread bars. If you think of them as more like a Luna bar with ingredients from home, then you'll have a better sense of what to expect. I enjoyed them, but I can see how they wouldn't be for everyone. My husband despises walnuts, so I used salted sunflower seeds instead of flaked salt or walnuts, to good effect.
Thanks! I’ve wanted a guideline to make such breakfast foods. I don’t add any sweeteners beyond fruit, so i’ll remove honey and add in some soaked dried fruit
Perfect gluten free recipe for soft bananas.
I wondered given the ingredients about the calorie count.
Bland. Neither sweet nor salty nor nutty.

I followed the recipe exactly, and I also found it lack in taste. I think the culprit is the peanut butter. Next time I will use a roasted peanut butter such as Trader’s Joe’s. Reduce the amount of olive oil, it prevents the squares from firming up.

Cal 388 cal

Protein 9.9 g

Carbs 30.3 g
Fiber 3.48 g
Sugars 15.08 g

Fat 26.8 g
Saturated fat 3.86 g
Unsaturated fat 13.6 g

Cholesterol 53 mg
Sodium 133 mg
Potassium 158 mg
I like this recipe, it's a kind of cakey Lara bar. I would leave out the salt next time, i just wanted the sweet flavors.

I would add some raisins or other dried fruits chopped up and maybe some orange zest and vanilla extract.
I like the suggestions for applesauce instead of oil.  We’ve already got some fat via the nut butter.
Just made this with defrosted frozen very ripe bananas. Delish! Didn’t have enough honey on hand, so added a half cup of mini choc chips on the top to sweeten. Also cut salt a bit. Delish!
Pretty bland, needs maybe more cinnamon or chocolate chips?
This was overly salty for me, and I love salt. I will cut back to half a teaspoon of kosher salt next time.

Followed recipe almost exactly. Only deviation was I added 2 tablespoons of coconut flour. These are delicious and low in sugar. I put the walnuts on top with the salt flakes and that for me makes them a stand out snack.  I for sure will make again!!!

I was JUST trying to find something for Bananas and oats this morning and how great was it that NYC cooking posted this :) We enjoyed it, I added a little bit of coconut sugar and chocolate chips instead of nuts based off comments and we enjoyed it! I think the only adjustment I would try next time is whizzing the oats in a blender to make it smoother (I've got a kiddo who struggles with texture sometimes). Otherwise it will be back on our bfast rotation!
I used almond butter, left out the olive oil, used maple instead of honey and did one banana and the rest mashed roasted sweet potato. Increased the oats by 20 grams. Heavenly.

I love these…but it helped to just barely toast the walnuts first. They add a lot of depth, and yes I’m getting the caramel notes and fudgy texture. Didn’t find them bland, but I also used frozen and thawed extremely overripe bananas. Maybe that helped!
Mine came out really salty. Otherwise, they were good.

Made earlier this week! My 7 year old loves them and I feel like there is plenty of flavor- definitely used old bananas. After days of her not wanting any breakfast I know she has some good energy for her school day.

These were delicious and so easy to make! The bars have a perfect balance of sweet and salt, great texture with oatmeal, and the banana comes through just enough. Perfect with coffee, or as an afternoon snack. Bonus: my son loves them too. I subbed 4tbsp apple sauce for oil bc that's what I had, dropped honey to 4tbsp, used almond butter, and added 1/2c unsweetened coconut bc why not? Will definitely make these again!
Don't skimp on the salt. It really needs it. I used peanuts on top due to tree nut sensitvities.

This was so fun and it tasted great, even after I completely screwed up the order of additions to the banana egg mixture. 

I threw all the next ingredients in to the bow except the flaky salt and it still ended up tasting great. I will do it right next time.

Easy grab for breakfast through the week. Win Win
These have taste but they are pretty salty. I'd make them again, but with about 1/2 the same. Definitely would skip the sprinkle on top
I added extra vanilla, halved the salt, and did half honey and half brown sugar, but more than recipe called for as many commenters said it was too salty and not sweet enough
Delicious! I used natural peanut butter so the flaked salt on top was the perfect finishing note.

These are delicious if you aren't expecting a sweet breakfast bar. I wonder if the author meant to say "unsalted" peanut butter, because the amount of salt is way too much if you use peanut butter with salt. I have taken others' suggestions and am using part olive oil and part applesauce to make it less rich an a bit sweeter.

I don't understand other reviewers complaints? I feel their bananas may not have been ripe enough or they they did not let the egg banana mixture sit long enough, because this was really good and nicely sweetened. Subbed the 4 tbsps of oil for 4 tbsps of apple sauce and reduced the PB to 1/2 cup. Would definitely make again!
After reading the comments, I made the recipe as is and then made a blueberry sauce to pour on top after serving. I enjoyed it with the sauce and might make it again. A good breakfast recipe.
Used coconut oil instead of olive, just 1/2 tsp salt (skipped the flaked salt on top), and it came out perfectly! Will absolutely make again- super easy way to use up some browning bananas!
I halved the salt and cinnamon; I loved them. Will make again!
good but a little oily for my taste in baked oatmeal bars.

Nice flavour and texture, but like others I found these a tad too salty AND too sweet. I’d recommend using 1/2 to 3/4 of the honey and only a 1 tsp of salt. It’s easy to overdo it with the sprinkling salt. To remedy this, I also sprinkled some chunky granulated sugar on top… sweet and salty! I used a mix of nut/seed butters (pistachio, sunflower and tahini) to spice up the flavours a bit. Peanut butter everything is so 2011…

My family loved these! Based on other comments, I used unsalted almond butter, omitted the flaky salt on top, and added a handful of mini chocolate chips. Ours had just the right amount of salt, and they’re very banana-y. Maybe bringing the eggs to room temperature first made a difference too? Anyway, we’ll make these again!
I read the Notes after the batter was mixed. Added raisins but the salt was already in. I ate it warm and thought it was delicious.

I saw a lot of comments saying that there was far too much salt, but I used the full amount of salt, made the recipe as written, and did not find it too salty. I found that these were not very sweet at all, but I don't want a lot of sugar in the morning so I personally really like them and will be making them on a regular basis! 
The sweetness/saltiness also might vary depending on the type of peanut butter you are using, I use all natural peanut butter that contains only peanuts and salt.

A few changed: I did half coconut oil / Half olive oil. I halved the walnut salt and put no flaky salt on top. I added 1/8tsp nutmeg, 1/8 cup raisins, and about 1/8 cup buckwheat flour. It was plenty sweet and not too bad!

I could not agree more with American in Paris. I cooked the recipe exactly as described - everything about the it  turned out heavy handed with a dull result, just like when you mix all colors and get a dull brown,  Pass on this one,  but if you must, reduce the peanut butter by half, use stone cut oatmeal and cook it 45-50 minutes (test at 40 minutes, as the reduction in peanut butter may reduce cooking time.
I too cut the honey down and decreased oil to 5 T (and added 1/3 c chocolate chips).  To make as 12 muffins: oven temp to 375F, bake for 23-25 min.

These were great! They're good for having on hand to eat quickly in the morning without having to rely on commerical granola or other breakfast bars. I did reduce the salt to 1 tsp and no sprinkle. Added 1/4 c mini chocolate chips but they weren't necessary. Surprisingly filling. Note - no flour in these!

I made these with a few modifications. I used almond butter (150 grams not the amount written). Added two large handfuls of chocolate chips but would add more next time (approx half cup). Was generous with vanilla. Skipped the salt on top. I was worried these wouldn’t be good given the reviews but my kids enjoyed them.

I made these with a few modifications. I used almond butter (150 grams not the amount written). Added two large handfuls of chocolate chips but would add more next time (approx half cup). Was generous with vanilla. I was worried about these given the reviews but my kids liked them!
These are great and tasty!!!  I used almond oil and Skippy crunchy peanut butter and made no other changes.

I loved this, as did everyone I shared it with, followed recipe exactly except for only 1 tsp salt due to using salted almond butter, hits the spot. I cut in into 12 squares and shared for pre skiing energy, perfect and lasted until lunch.

Despite the harsh comments, I really enjoyed these! They reminded me of banana pancakes and had a lovely texture. I added golden raisins bc I had them and if I make these again I’ll add shredded coconut and maybe increase the oats. Thanks Sohla!
I made this recipe as directed and found it perfect - it is meant to be salty/sweet. Delicious. Especially the day after making. Highly recommend as a snacking cake.

I thought this recipe was actually great. I actually added 3 bananas (352 g), less oil, a few more oats (130 g), less honey,  and got spiritual with the salt. Because I am pregnant, I also added psyllium husk powder, lol. Delicious and fudgy.

I made these last night and kind of agree that they taste like nothing, but not in a bad enough way that I won’t make & eat them for a quick breakfast on busy mornings.  Some modifications I made:
-double batch in a 13x9 pan bc I had 4 bananas I had to use up (only had about 410 grams but was okay) 
-subbed plain Greek yogurt for ALL of the oil to reduce calories & add protein
-used an immersion blender for all but the oats 
-left out the walnuts, I hate nuts in my baked goods

Agreed that these are far too salty as called for especially if you use salted peanut butter. Just a pinch is needed. Cut sweetener down to just one teaspoon. We enjoy them as a snack or for breakfast!

I made a few changes, and it's yummy!

4 table spoon of olive oil.
3/4 cup of brown sugar (instead of honey)
1 tea spoon of vanilla
a pinch of salt
I made these with peanut butter. My husband thought they were awesome, but the kids didn't like them.
Wow, cannot believe that people find these bland! They are moist and delicious! Yummy PB flavor! Making again!
10 April 2023.

Used this recipe as a starting point and added some spices after reading the other comments about blandness. Tossed in some almonds, sunflower seeds and dried fruit. Substituted flax "eggs" for eggs. The final result had a good flavor and texture, and could have used some chocolate.

this does not taste like banana and it’s salty even though I skipped the salt on top 
I don’t mind the flavor but the olive oil makes it savory.  I would tweak this with less salt.
I substituted coconut oil
Used half the olive oil, honey, and salt recommended in the recipe. Added plumped raisins and bits of a chocolate bar. Tastes good but definitely don’t expect it to be like a dessert.
I'm always on the hunt for a low-ish sugar option for breakfast with all the good stuff (bananas, oats, nuts) and this one fits the bill.  Delicious!
Maybe use unsalted nut butter?  Way too salty!

These were just OK; 
Should have punted and made banana bread.

Good, super easy. I was a little weary given all the notes after i had already put this in the oven.  I added some coconut flakes, some mini choc chip, and mixed in the walnuts.  I was a little unsure of how soft this would be because the batter was very very loose.  I added a couple of tablespoons of gluten free flour and it came out great and very moist.  Easy to cut. 
Would make it again the same way.

I really like this recipe and will make again. I don’t think it’s bland like some commenters, has a nice banana nut flavor. In the future, will cut back on salt, 2 tsp is way too much. Will add extra nuts on top and maybe mix in some dried fruit (cranberry, apricot, or date).
only needs 1/2 tsp of salt, otherwise salt is the only thing you can taste
Could you use prunes instead of bananas?

I made these today varied in two respects. 1st reduced the oil to 4 Tablespoons. 6 is too much with addition of the peanut butter or almond butter for all you fancy folks out there. The other I did was add another 1/2 cup of oats. 1 and 1/2 cup of oats overall gave more consistency to recipe and added a little more substance into the batter. This recipe firmed up nicely. I will say that I did not follow the ingredient sequence to the T and it still turned out.

I feel like this is some kind of experiment to test to see if people will notice when one ingredient in a list is waaaay out of proportion. But yeah, way too much salt, which is more annoying if you kinda thought that as you were making, but didn't trust yourself. I need more Martha Rose!

Made this for a 10x15 pan. Made no substitutions except obviously doubling the ingredients and adding pecans instead of walnuts. Bar baked beautifully but definitely salty like everyone else is saying. Reduce salt by half for sure!
I thought these were great. Added chocolate chips and next time will add hemp seeds and chia seeds. Wasn’t bland at all. Skipped the salt on top, based on others’ complaint of saltiness.
Delicious, flavorful and super moist. Making them for the third time today to satisfy a craving.

The salt ratio in this recipe is wrong! It should always be 1:2 kosher salt: fine salt. Making it as listed results in it being too salty.

Adjusting the salt, I love this recipe but think of it as a base to riff on. Definitely need to add nuts, dried fruit, seeds, chocolate chips, coconut, or anything else you have on hand that could go on a granola bar.
Yes, these are WAY too salty.  Half or 3/4 of a teaspoon instead of two full teaspoons would have been much better.

I made this with less salt and honey as many suggested. I also added various nuts and seeds to the batter which helped with texture quite a bit. Between the oats, banana, peanut butter, and nuts, this had nice flavor. I enjoyed these quite a bit. Reminded me of banana bread. 

I even made it a second time with homemade apple sauce instead of bananas. Less satisfying than with bananas. Next time I’ll try both fruits. 

Perfect homemade, healthy, fiber rich, grab and go breakfast
Followed the recipe; it made a gooey, heavy bar. We didn't enjoy it.

These were delicious, but I'm glad I read the notes first. I reduced the oil to 3 tablespoons and did 4 tablespoons of honey, 2 of maple syrup. I added a few dark chocolate chips and did cinnamon sugar instead of just cinnamon. They came out great, but I imagine they'd be super greasy with more oil, so definitely reduce. They were super flavorful--I'm surprised so many people mentioned no flavor! Maybe the maple syrup helped.

These are great. I riffed on them from the first, adding coconut flakes, poppyseeds, coco powder, skipped nut topping most of the time. A great base for playing around with. Does anyone know if we really have to let it sit? And if so, could they add that to the recipe?? My brain needs to know whyyyyy!!!
the second time i made these i got it right. I pre roasted some almonds and cashews, and added a handful of top notch belgian dark chocolate chips for good measure. thank you Sohla, this is a keeper.
I followed the recipe but at the end mixed walnuts into the batter instead of just on top. Perfection! Mine were not bland; maybe the use of very overripe bananas helped.
I added store bought pecan pralines to the top 5 min before they were done instead of the other nuts. Would recommend!

Loved it! This was super easy and flavorful. Only two modifications: 1) I ended up with almost two cups of bananas because I had four going bad at the same time and 2) I added 1/2 cup chocolate chips. I didn’t experience the blandness (maybe the chocolate chips helped), the lack of banana flavor (maybe 1 cup as listed in the recipe isn’t enough), or saltiness (I like things on the saltier side so this was a bonus) that others commented on.
These are incredibly salty - next time I'd only use a half teaspoon.

Remember to mix egg and banana and let sit. 
Reduce salt to 1tsp. 
Reduce honey to 1/4 cup. 
Add chocolate chips.
I only had fine sea salt, used 1/2 tsp after reading the comments. Plenty sweet and not too salty.

I found these to be quite oily. Reducing oil, adding additional oats and some additional liquid would have made this a better overall recipe. Agree with those who recommend cranberries & perhaps skip the salt on top.

I loved these, with modifications based on other’s comments. I reduced oil to 5 Tbsp and salt to 3/4 tsp, with no added salt on top. I eliminated honey and added no sugar to recipe, but did add raisins. Put a mixture of walnuts and pecans on top. Came out beautifully moist after 30 minutes with a rich peanut butter/oatmeal taste. I will make these again.
9 servings at 391 cal. each.
would decrease sweetener ( we used maple syrup) and decrease salt
The nutrition info is shown in the drop down list located underneath the list of Ingredients.
Honestly starting to grow on me! Thomas really liked them too
These are great and Cecilia likes them too. Go with 3 T each honey and oil, and 1/2 t table salt.
Way too salty will try one more time using unsalted nut butter, and maybe add another 2 tablespoons of honey.
Regarding feedback saying it’s bland — anybody try going to 3 bananas, maybe other nuts (pecans), etc.?
Can one omit the olive oil altogether? One serving of this has more fat than my usual full day total. Would applesauce or flaxseed meal replace the eggs and the oil to further reduce the fat?

Agree with others that the olive oil is a strange addition to a breakfast cereal bar. It seems to drown out the banana flavor entirely. Next time I’ll use melted butter. And the addition of dried cranberries or raisins would add a bright bit of chewiness and sweetness

I made this but did not use any salt
Added 1/2 cup golden raisins, 1teas of ground chia & same amount  of ground flax seed
They are tasty, moist and delicious!!!
Nothing bland about this recipe 
Used 9x9 pan lined with parchment 
At higher elevation so i took it out after 30 minutes
It was crispy around the sides-easy to cut
Will make thus again!!
Thanks for this deliciousness

OMG!!! These will be addicting if I don't watch out! I used about 3 1/2 T of honey and added mini chocolate chips. I used half the salt and it seemed like enough for me. Not bland at all...I really loved them!

I followed the recipe exactly as written and they turned out a soft mess, even after 45 minutes in the oven. The tester came out clean but they just wouldn't set. There is nothing to bind them other than the oats, and they don't really bind anyway. If I was to try them again, and I won't, I'd add a half cup of flour.

Wow!  What a range of reactions!  Same for my household. Made the recipe exactly. Shared with my husband who told me nobody would ever ask for the recipe. I was neutral on the result, questioning if I had undercooked it. Rereading the intro, the word “fudgy” jumped out. Yes!  These are not like coffee cake or granola bars. Since I will not waste ingredients or my effort, I have been eating them each morning or for a late afternoon snack. They satisfying and curb my appetite. Will make again!
These were delicious - but too salty. I would halve the salt next time.
These worked well for me with some alterations, subbed pb powder for the peanut butter, used chocolate chips in place of honey and added some apple sauce.  Also def half the salt!

These aren't as bad as some of the comments suggest. That said, I think if I make 'em again, I would use either butter or veg oil in place of the olive oil, and omit the flaked salt on top. Maybe add a couple of tablespoons of flour? Other than that, I liked the peanut butter/banana/oat combo.

Loved this. Reading the other comments, I bumped up the spices- powdered ginger, and a bit of cloves and allspice. I have trouble with honey and used maple syrup instead. It tastes delicious. I can taste a hint of banana, but not so much the peanut butter. It’s hefty and just right for breakfast or a solid afternoon snack.
Delicious, but I’ll use half the oil next time. Plenty of moisture with the peanut butter.
Delicious! Dimensional and filling, perfect with afternoon coffee.

Used maple syrup. Misread directions and mixed in nuts instead of putting on top. I used slivered almonds. They were super moist and almost too sweet for my liking.. one day in Tupperware they were almost bread like, moist.
My toddler and i devoured them as snack and breakfast bars. Cooked them again 3 days later just to have in the freezer!

Love these. Froze most of them and pull them out as needed. Swapped 4 tbsp apple butter (homemade) for 4 tbsp oil, replaced honey with 1 tbsp maple sugar, and added ¼ cup each coconut flakes, carob nibs, and dried cranberries. Used defrosted frozen bananas. Baked 30 minutes. Very hearty.
Just made these. They are really good. I followed the recipe as it is published. They will be the perfect breakfast bar. For the honey I used Hero’s Orange, a filtered orange honey.
I was expecting more crunch on the exterior portions, they were tasty enough. I am a novice baker, but even I thought that 6 tbsp of olive oil was too much.
Followed the recipe and these turned out a little salty and bland to my taste. That said, it’s an easy treat to make and enjoy. Might try adding chocolate chips and less salt if I make them again.

Not sure about all the negative comments, I quite liked these! We reduced the salt by half per suggestion and added crushed pecans and chopped toffee on top instead of walnuts. It was delicious, I couldn’t stop eating them!
If this recipe was updated on January 17, 2024, what were the updated and how will the update impact the comments from last year?

This is a great breakfast bar, versus a desert. It is everything that people are commenting negatively on… salty, not sweet, etc. But that makes for a lovely breakfast treat. I made mine with almond butter and added a bit of unsweetened coconut. Perfect. Next time I may try to add some protein powder too.

Not sure why there are so many negative comments. I used 2.5 very ripe bananas, subbed 4 T of applesauce for 4 T of the oil (still used 2 T oil, but I was also worried about the high fat content), used almond butter, and added 1/2 cup of chocolate chips - and they came out delicious! Both my 6 yo daughter and very particular husband loved them. Tasted the bananas, didn’t detect too much salt, and loved the fudge texture. This was a win for us! Easier way to use up bananas than banana bread.
These were delicious!!! I couldn’t stop eating  them. I used natural peanut butter and no walnuts, followed the recipe. It’s also a perfect and nutritious snack if you’re breastfeeding :)

A lot of the flavor of this bar comes from peanut butter. Does your peanut butter have salt? Is your peanut butter already sweetened? Does your peanut butter have your favorite PEANUT taste? Do you prefer other nut butters? Maybe you should use your favorite ___ butter in this recipe. Same with bananas - did you use seriously ripe banana that smelled strongly of banana flavor? I substitute out honey for a preferred syrup combined with solid coconut oil (melted). Use FRESH/raw walnuts for flavor.
These definitely tasted better on day 2 as flavor deepened. I used less salt and added chocolate chips on mine.

These are very good. The negative reviewers may have been expecting something more like banana bread, which these are not. We tried them because we need an alternative to our morning oatmeal routine. Used maple syrup instead of honey, toasted pecans instead of walnuts and added raisins as some reviewers recommended. They are not overly sweet, but are delicious with a cup of coffee and berries for breakfast. The nut butter adds a protein punch and keeps hunger away until lunch.

Made these last night and ate this morning, honestly they are yummy and super moist!  I followed the recipe almost exactly, using unsalted peanut butter.  I did cut the salt and still found them salty.  So I would definitely cut the salt in half, more if your nut butter has salt.  Added just a few chocolate chips on top instead of walnuts because that’s what I had.

Followed verbatim.  Not flavorless at all, rather the taste of peanut butter and olive oil, which while not unpleasant, wasn’t pleasant.   Maybe it was the brand of peanut butter. Too many other recipes to try versus working on modding this one.

Yes, I agree with everyone else, 2 tsp salt is way too much; one tsp is plenty. Next time, I'll use less olive oil and add some sugar or maple syrup. 
Pumpkin seeds on top in addition to the walnuts is nice too.

Disappointing recipe. I cut the honey to 4T, significantly cut the salt and probably did half the nut butter. Had a long cook time, baked for 40 minutes- edges were browning almost too much but center still wasn’t done. Taste was okay but too oily. If I make these again I will try applesauce and cut the oil. Took this recipe out of my recipe box. Agree with others, effort and ingredients weren’t worth the recipe.
Way too sweet. Probably don’t need any honey, let alone 4 TBs. Maybe sub in some unsweetened coconut.

Adding some love to balance out all the haters here, because I LOVE this recipe. It’s definitely not for everyone, because no, it’s not sweet — it’s salty-sweet-savory. But if you recognize the ruse that banana bread pulled off by convincing millions that it is not a cake but a breakfast food, and if you also would prefer your breakfast more on the savory side than the sweet, then this is the overripe banana recipe for you! It’s become my weekday breakfast of choice.
I used a combination of peanut and almond butter, out of necessity. Overall there was little flavor except oat and salt, and I omitted the salt on top. Just ok.
I added nutmeg and cloves and cranberries and chocolate to enrich flavor.  Yum!  Pecans worked better than walnuts.

I did not have Diamond Crystal so I cut the salt back to 1 tsp.  I subbed in avocado oil because I had some I wanted to use up, cut the honey back to 100 grams, used a natural peanut butter (only dry roasted peanuts and salt), used very ripe bananas and cut the squares into 16 instead of 9.  Both my husband and I thought these were pretty yummy.

Not sure what all the negativity is about. My husband is allergic to peanuts so I made the bars with almond butter. Followed the rest of the recipe as written. The bars came out moist and flavorful. Perfect for a quick breakfast.

This isn’t banana bread - it is more of a moist peanut cake. Made as directed, a bit leery of all the oil and salt. Very oily, salty peanut flavor, but not the worst thing I’ve ever made. Agree with comments to reduce oil and salt, maybe add some chocolate chip. Use neutral oil instead of olive oil. Maybe mix the nuts into the batter next time.

I followed the recipe and used almond butter. I am surprised at the comments as I found the bars to  be very tasty, although I did not taste  the almond butter. It is exciting to find a desert recipe with no flour or sugar which are inflammatory ingredients. I used to be a pastry chef and LOVE baked goods. But I  prefer to concentrate on healthier foods the majority of the time . I plan on making  this recipe again and experiment with persimmons and apples.
Omit salt except 1/4 tsp in batter.  Add 1/4-1/3 c dark raisins.  Use smooth peanut butter vs crunchy.

I made this as is but added chopped chocolate for a topping. It is greasy. It is sweet. It is salty. An absolute perfect pre x country ski breakfast or as a pre meal for any other long haul sport. Will keep engines running all morning.

Made a few times & a hit in our house with changes culled from others:
Added 1/4 cup oats, dark chocolate chips and pressed some toasted coconut into the top before baking (to keep it from falling off).
Reduced oil to 2 TBSP, salt to 1 tsp, Maple instead of honey. (Also, peanut vs almond butter weirdly didn’t taste much different and flax eggs vs real with no detectable difference.)
4 bananas over ripe
Followed the recipe exactly. The texture threw me off. It's like banana bread. I was expecting it to be more firm.
These were delicious but salty. The recipe called for WAY too much salt so I cut it down to 1 tsp although next time I’ll only use 1/4 tsp. Sprinkled chopped walnuts on top.

These are easy to make and are great to have available for breakfast all week. Followed the recipe except used maple syrup instead of honey, almond butter, and used only 4 TBSP oil (I was worried my almond butter was too oily). Mine came out a bit dry so next time would use the full 6 TBSP. For anyone who thinks the banana flavor is lost - make sure to weigh your ingredients, my bananas were small so I ended up needing 3 (and still only got to 220g).

Made this yesterday and my husband I I polished off two bars each this morning. Neither of us understand the hate for this recipe. I halved the honey and added nutmeg   cloves, and left everything else as written. Filling, tasty, not too sweet.  Will make again. Travels well for breakfast on the go.

We thought they were amazing! Reduced salt to 1/2 tsp and added an extra 1/4 cup of oats. I don’t know why people are saying they have no flavor—I thought they were full of flavor! This recipe will be on repeat for us.

I liked these but the consistency was less cake than I'd hoped.  The flavor was good but somewhat bland, neither sweet nor salty.  It tasted too much peanut butter so perhaps 3/4 c peanut butter is a bit too much and the almonds made it very nutty.  I wouldn't rush to make it again but I agree that adding chocolate chips would give it nice flavor.

I made these pretty much to the recipe with the exception of cutting back on the salt by half as well as the honey. I also mixed the walnuts in the batter so added some extra on the top after noticing my mistake. I did not add salt to the top. One thing I should have taken into consideration was that my peanut butter had no sugar. The texture was fine but as others have commented they were boring. I will make again and use the original amount of salt, perhaps use agave syrup-lower GI than honey.
Added cinnamon sugar to the top at the end instead of salt, and also added chocolate chips to the mixture. Turned out really good and my friends liked them as well. Good gluten free option.

Pecans substitute
325 convection  fo 45 min

While I don’t hate these, they are not a favourite. I read comments after I made them and have to agree that there is too much salt and not enough of a dominant flavour. If I make them again I think I will add dried fruit and maybe stir in some extra nuts.

Anything with cinnamon I quadruple the amount. I halved the honey and used hemp sead oil. Without my extravagant use of cinnamon I can see how they would be bland. I love the idea of adding chia. Overall, I'm a fan!

This is the second time I have made this recipe. The first time it turned out perfectly and it got amazing raves from friends.  It was moist and dense like a brownie but banana bread instead.  

To adjust the protein/carb/fat ratio I made the following changes:
4 egg whites for the eggs
Mixed nut butter for the PB
1/2c vanilla protein powder   1/2 c ancient grains GF flour. No oats
No honey or sweetener.

The batter tastes incredible so far.

Based on other comments, I decreased the oil and the honey - but I stirred in about a quarter cup of chocolate hazelnut spread. : ) No cinnamon, so I added a dash of allspice and about a half teaspoon of ginger. Husband just went back for another piece.

I was nearly discouraged from trying this recipe by all the negative comments. But I really like these bars. They’re not sweet, which is exactly what I want in a breakfast option. They have heft thanks to the oats and peanut butter. And a nice crunch and nuttiness from the walnuts. The salt complaints, I would guess, come down to Morton vs Diamond Crystal (or other brands) — I had no problems using Diamond in the amounts recommended. I’d make this recipe again and happily enjoy it!

Wasn't sure what to expect from this given the split between the 4-star overall rating, and a number of negative comments, but tried it anyway, and quite liked it! Mine came out tasting of both bananas & peanut butter, with a very soft consistency ("fudgy," as described). Easy. Good recipe to have on hand for using up ripe bananas. Made exactly as suggested except I used cashews on top because I was out of walnuts.

I should have read the comments first. Even after trusting my intuition and cutting the salt by 1/4tsp these were inedibly salty. That's the only flavor they have, actually. Somehow, the saltiness was enhanced over night and we had to throw them out. This is the third time now I've made a NYT Coking recipe that called for ludicrous salt. What's the deal? Going to try again but omit the salt except for a topping and add some chocolate chips. I think there's a decent recipe here it just needs help
I reduced honey to 4T and threw in a bunch of mini chocolate chips. Then mixed the walnuts in with the batter and topped with sliced almonds. Delicious
Too salty. Next time I will cut the salt in half and maybe add some chocolate chips as some have suggested here.
My son doesn’t like peanut butter so I subbed tahini. Came out great!

I read the notes first, and then made the following modifications: used a combination of almond butter and peanut butter, cut salt by half (used sea salt though), doubled the vanilla, used a mix of cardamom and cinnamon, cut the honey to 3 T, and added less than 1/4 cu dark chocolate chips.

Smells wonderful, tastes good.  It did not come out oily.  It would have been even more flavorful if my bananas were a bit riper.  It’s not my favorite baked good but it’s tasty and a lot healthier.

These aren’t bad, but two tablespoons of salt is way too much, and I like my baked goods with some salty flavor in them. A bit bland (used almond butter, not PB), and if I hadn’t added chocolate chunks I don’t think I would have liked them. I also used a very fruity olive oil with great flavor, but the other ingredients (mostly the chocolate) drowned it out. If I did it over, I’d use more banana, half the salt, peanut butter and less expensive olive oil.
Recipe calls for 2 tsp, not 2 Tb of salt!
These weren’t bad but neither were they great. The flavors are muddled, the texture heavy. Meh.

I liked the idea of this recipe (whole grains, healthy oils, natural sweeteners) but it fell short of my taste expectations. I have made this twice and I agree with other reviews that say the bars are bland. The first time I followed the recipe. The batter was thin and the finished bars were oily. I switched to sunflower oil and cut it back to 2 tablespoons the second time, but the bars were a bit dry and again the batter was thin. I'm an experienced home cook who started baking in the 1970's.

I made these today despite the poor reviews, and they came out fine. I decreased the salt, and  also used *unsalted* almond butter. I suspect many of the reviews saying the recipe is too salty might be because folks are using salted peanut butter. I decreased the honey to 4 Tbsp and added about 1/4 C of hemp seeds. I also used a hand blender to combine the mashed banana and eggs; chunks of banana would make the bars "claggy" as one review put it. Overall, a nice flour free breakfast item.

I thought the flavors of this were good. I skipped sprinkling salt on top based on the comments and subbed the cinnamon with British mixed spice that I had leftover from another recipe. My biggest complaint is with the texture. It's very loose.
So simple and easy to make! Came out super fudgey and moist. Great healthier alternative to banana breads with loads of gluten and sugar!
I accidentally left out the salt (except the flaky salt on top) and thought they were great! Added some bittersweet chocolate chips too. Delicious!

I made this before reading the reviews. I think it depends what you expect…it tastes good with unsweetened unsalted peanut butter and is moist and breakfasty. It’s not a cake or dessert. Glad I trusted my instinct and halved the salt but otherwise followed recipe to a T. Not sure I’d run out to made it again but the reviews seem overly negative
These tasted better than expected based on the reviews but the texture is very craggy. Wouldn’t make again. Somehow too sweet and would be much too salty with full amount of salt

To me, the most prominent flavors here are salt and peanut butter. I used weight measures to make this, and my bananas were ripe. I used Morton’s kosher salt, and my peanut butter was homemade with lightly salted peanuts. I left the flaked salt off the top, and this was still too salty for my liking. This isn’t something I need to rush to make again.
I found this recipe unusual! The bars work well for me as a substantial, nutritious and tasty portable breakfast. They hover in an interesting space between sweet and savory, leaning toward sweet.

I read the reviews about it being too salty and bland. So, I left out the salt, was a little heavy handed on the honey, let the bananas over-ripen, and used pumpkin spice instead of cinnamon for extra kick. It turned out great- very fudgy, sweet enough, and dense/filling. I think I could up the spice by a half teaspoon- but it’s very good as is.

Messed around with the recipe due to comments about bland flavor. Added some greek yogurt, chocolate chips, and extra PB. Cooked closer to 55min. I love that there is no flour/baking powder in this. Feels more substantial for breakfast and less of a mess on my counter.

This is a great base recipe that is easy to enhance to your personal taste.  Try adding fruit like blueberries or tart cherries (buy packaged dried, then rehydrate).  Additional spices like ginger or cardamon are also good.  Adjusting the quantity of rolled oats and/or adding the chopped nuts directly to the mixture will change the texture of the bars.  I enjoy them with morning coffee right from the freezer and thawed just enough to eat with a fork.  Yum!
Added  the chocolate chips as someone suggested! Really liked them!

I cooked with recipes from the NY Times all the time, some recipes have not been entirely to our liking but as a whole they are really good. This recipe particular recipe was terrible, barely eatable, tasted too much like peanut butter and it was so bland that I threw the whole thing out in the garbage.
We were disappointed.  All the ingredients sounded like they would work together, but nope.  Lesson learned, read the notes/comments.

Supremely disappointing. Crumbly, too cakey, salty, little of the sweetness I’d expected, nut butter and banana didn’t come through, even the oats were lost in the breadiness. I MIGHT try it again, but this batch is like eating some kind of medicinal, eat-your-vegetables blah.
Agree with the less than glowing comments - not worth the calories to eat something lacking in taste.

Very salt forward. No to little banana flavor. Not much peanut butter flavor either. I used salted PB.   The recipe didn’t specify if it should be salted or not. But the added salt was a lot. So keep this in mind! Add more banana, half salt and unsalted PB in my opinion.

These came together really easily but the flavor was off for me. The peanut butter dominated (in a bad way) and the texture was more cake-like than bar-like. There was little to no banana flavor and the salty-sweet elements almost canceled each other out.
Note to self: go easy on the salt here. It’s not like a brownie or a choc chip cookie where the more salt the better, banana things can be too salty very quickly

3T olive oil, 3T applesauce
6T peanut butter, 6T applesauce
